The Role will report directly to the Head to Anti\-Financial Crime, Ireland and serves as a subject matter expert (SME) playing a critical role in safeguarding our European businesses against financial crime risks. Sitting within the second line Anti‑Financial Crime (AFC) function, this role carries significant responsibility in supporting Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services (FAS) business in the execution of the Financial Crime framework (which consists of AML and Terrorist financing, Sanctions, Bribery and Corruption and Tax Evasion) during the distribution of Vanguard UCITS range and oversight of Irish Fund's Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter\-Terrorist Financing (CFT) programme. **Core Responsibilities** * Support the maintenance and enhancement of the European AFC Framework, including methodology updates, documentation improvements, and alignment to group‑wide standards. * Advise the FAS business in respect to compliance with the Vanguard global financial crime policy requirements including local European obligations. * Create, update, and manage the Anti Financial Crime policies, procedures, and supplements. * In collaboration with the UK AFC team execute an assurance program for the areas that Vanguard Europe insources and support the creation of specific financial crime testing programs for oversight of outsourced third\-party relationships. * Review and analyse escalations in respect to due diligence, screening and transaction monitoring alerts. Resolves exceptions, including unusual and complex issues escalated by more junior team members, according to established guidelines. Collaborates with managers to resolve the most complex issues. * In collaboration with the UK AFC team execute of the Vanguard Europe annual risk assessments methodology for AML, Sanctions and Bribery and Corruption including detailed data gathering, quantitative and qualitative analysis, drafting of risk narratives, and coordination of validation and challenge with stakeholders across the First and Second line of defence. * Support the submission of periodic regulatory and internal AFC reporting for Vanguard Europe and the FAS business, ensuring all submissions are accurate, timely, complete, and delivered according to defined governance and regulatory expectations. * Assist project governance by helping prepare papers, progress updates, risk logs, and milestone reporting. * Provide formal and informal educational activities, including presentations, staff meetings and training programs. Develops and delivers financial crimes related training and education for Vanguard Europe crew members. * Maintains a comprehensive knowledge of the investment industry and compliance developments with implications for Vanguard's financial crimes program. Keeps abreast of the current market, regulatory and industry news. Serve as a consultant on compliance related issues. Acts as a liaison to regulatory agencies.Strong understanding of Anti Money Laundering/Counter Terrorist financing, sanctions and Anti Bribery and Corruption requirements, risk, regulatory expectations, industry practice, business systems and processes in Ireland and the UK. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Significant practical experience delivering or coordinating risk assessments, including data analysis, narrative writing, and validation stages. * Demonstrated experience managing regulatory or internal reporting cycles, including drafting, coordinating, and quality‑assuring submissions. * Experience working with policy governance, supporting policy refresh cycles, or maintaining standards frameworks. * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with experience drafting governance papers, risk assessments, or policy content. * High attention to detail, strong organisational skills, and ability to work under pressure in a fast‑paced environment. * Ability to interpret regulatory expectations and apply them pragmatically. **Desirable** * Experience operating across multiple European jurisdictions and/or global AFC structures. * Experience of supporting the distribution of an Irish UCITS range. * Exposure to transformation or change programmes (e.g., technology enhancements, operating model changes). **Qualifications:** * Minimum 5\+ years’ experience in AML/CFT, compliance, audit, or risk within financial services. Management Company/Asset Management 2nd Line AFC experience preferred. * Professional qualification relating to Financial Crime / Undergraduate degree or equivalent combination of training and experience **Special Factors:** * This position follows a hybrid working model that will require you to be in our Dublin office three days per week. * Vanguard is not offering visa sponsorship for this position. **How We Work** Vanguard has implemented a hybrid working model for the majority of our crew members, designed to capture the benefits of enhanced flexibility while enabling in\-person learning, collaboration, and connection. We believe our mission\-driven and highly collaborative culture is a critical enabler to support long\-term client outcomes and enrich the employee experience.

title: Financial Planning Advisor
description: **Fairstone Ireland** is seeking applications from experienced Financial Planning Advisors for full\-time permanent opportunities nationwide. As a Financial Planning Advisor, you’ll provide expert, independent financial advice to a diverse client base, helping them achieve their financial goals through tailored solutions. This position involves managing an established client portfolio while also focusing on growing new business opportunities. You will have significant autonomy and the chance to shape financial plans that drive measurable results. This role is part of the Fitness Probity Regime and Individual Accountability Framework, which set out the specific requirements applicable. **Responsibilities** * Deliver professional financial advice that complies with the Central Bank of Ireland’s regulations and Fairstone’s best practices. * Manage and grow an existing portfolio of clients, ensuring a proactive and client\-focused service. * Conduct thorough research and analysis to recommend financial solutions that address a wide range of client needs. * Drive new business growth through referrals, self\-generated leads, and existing Fairstone client relationships. * Ensure all client records are maintained accurately, documenting objectives, goals, and recommendations. * Stay ahead of industry trends, regulatory changes, and market developments to provide informed advice. * Actively participate in business development initiatives, contributing to the growth of client Assets Under Management (AUM) and overall revenue. **The ideal candidate** * A minimum of 12 months' experience as a Financial Planning Advisor, with a deep knowledge of financial planning, including protection, pensions, investments, tax\-efficient strategies, and holistic planning. * A proven track record of managing and growing client portfolios. * A high level of commercial awareness and the ability to identify opportunities for business growth. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build trust and explain complex information clearly and concisely. * Strong organisational skills, with a focus on accuracy and attention to detail. * A client\-centric approach, prioritising strong, long\-term relationships and exceptional service delivery. *Qualifications* * Fully QFA qualified is an absolute requirement. CFP is a distinct advantage. * In\-depth knowledge of financial regulations, markets, and industry practices. * Strong leadership qualities and the ability to mentor junior advisors, if required. * Proficient in using financial planning software and tools. Please note that any offer of employment with Fairstone Ireland is subject to the satisfactory completion of pre\-employment checks. These checks include verification of your references, education and professional qualifications, confirmation of eligibility to work in the Republic of Ireland, and a review of publicly available professional/personal information (internet search). Employment will not commence until all checks have been completed to our satisfaction. For more information, please email **hr@fairstone.ie** and the HR team will be able to assist you further.

title: Financial Reporting, Off
description: **Who we are looking for:** Based in the Dublin office, you will be part of a team of 200\+ dedicated financial reporting professionals who are focused on delivering interim and annual US GAAP/IFRS financial statements to a diverse group of alternative investment managers. **Why this role is important to us:** The team you will be joining is a part of State Street Global Services (SSGS). SSGS gives asset owners and managers access to the essential financial tools they need to deliver effective investment solutions. From core custody, accounting, fund administration and shareholder recordkeeping, to complete operations solutions and servicing for alternative assets like OTC derivatives, private equity and real estate, SSGS’ tools help our clients make better investment choices and act on growth opportunities. Join us if making your mark in the financial services industry from day one is a challenge you are up for. **What you will be responsible for:** As a Financial Reporting Officer you will Plan, schedule, and manage the production of interim / annual financial statements and daily workflow for multiple alternative investment client relationships and product types (hedge, private equity, and real estate funds). * Act as the primary point of contact for clients with responsibility for implementing and supporting the service requirements of the various client relationships serviced within the group. * Perform technical reviews of hedge, private equity, and real estate funds’ financial statements in accordance with relevant GAAP and regulatory requirements. * Set individual and team targets and priorities; maintain high standards of client service; conduct performance appraisals and give feedback on individual and team performance. * Be a source of technical support and guidance, including reviewing financial statements prepared by your teams and ensuring that all financial statements are accurate and prepared in accordance with the relevant GAAP prior to issuance to external parties (clients / auditors). * Support new business integration and ensure operating models are designed to deliver best of breed service to clients leveraging global footprint as necessary. * Manage your own continued professional development, through engaging in the performance review process and attending relevant training as per agreed training curriculum. * Deputise for your assistant vice president as required from time to time. * Perform other duties as required from time to time. **What we value** These skills will help you succeed in this role * Excellent communicate skills * Strong leadership abilities. * Excellent attention to detail * Strong knowledge of accounting standards as they relate to funds (IFRS, Irish GAAP in particular) * Ability to multi\-task and be comfortable with managing difficult situations as they arise. * Be able to communicate effectively with colleagues, clients, auditors at all levels. * Experience of working to deadlines. * Ability to delegate work to others. * Ability to effectively represent the team to clients and internal stakeholders **Education \& Preferred Qualifications** * Possess a degree in finance, business / accounting or related discipline, * Have 6\-8 years’ experience working in a similar environment or related environment, including experience managing staff * The role would also suit someone who is a qualified professional accountant (eg. ACA, ACCA, CPA). * The candidate should possess strong knowledge of US GAAP/IFRS investment company financial statements with previous experience servicing alternative investments.. **Disposition** * Self\-motivated * Good initiative * Professional * Multi\-tasking ability * Enthusiastic * Adaptable – ability to develop new approaches to solving problems * Flexible * Strong inter\-personal skills **About State Street** **What we do.** State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. From technology to product innovation, we’re making our mark on the financial services industry. For more than two centuries, we’ve been helping our clients safeguard and steward the investments of millions of people. We provide investment servicing, data \& analytics, investment research \& trading and investment management to institutional clients. **Work, Live and Grow.** We make all efforts to create a great work environment. Our benefits packages are competitive and comprehensive. Details vary by location, but you may expect generous medical care, insurance and savings plans, among other perks. You’ll have access to flexible Work Programs to help you match your needs. And our wealth of development programs and educational support will help you reach your full potential. **Inclusion, Diversity and Social Responsibility.** We truly believe our employees’ diverse backgrounds, experiences and perspectives are a powerful contributor to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and reach their maximum potential while adding value to both our organization and our clients. We warmly welcome candidates of diverse origin, background, ability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity and personality. Another fundamental value at State Street is active engagement with our communities around the world, both as a partner and a leader. You will have tools to help balance your professional and personal life, paid volunteer days, matching gift programs and access to employee networks that help you stay connected to what matters to you. State Street is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. **About State Street** ====================== Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement

title: Finishing Foreperson
description: At Glenveagh, we're proud to build homes and support communities. And we build strong teams that enable us to do that. As one of Ireland's Best Large Workplaces in 2025, and the first construction company in Ireland to be accredited with the Gold Investors in Diversity mark, we create a workplace where everyone feels supported, and able to grow their career. We're looking for people who want to play their part in delivering homes to individuals and families, and all while availing of great benefits and the opportunity for long term career development. We are currently recruiting a Finishing Foreperson for our residential project in Mooretown, North County Dublin. **Responsibilities:** * Liaise and communicate closely with the design team, Site Manager and other stakeholders * Coordinate the daily resourcing and control of site labour and ordering of materials, completing the online daily site diary and relevant weekly documentation in accordance with company policy * Provide key point of contact for purchasers * Liaise closely with Sales * Coordinate snagging crews, overseeing effective completion of snag lists * Carry out company compliance and administration procedures and recording performance and progress of site operations and subcontractors * Effective planning of tasks and efficiently organising the plant and site facilities in order to meet deadlines * Ensure works are carried out in compliance with building regulations, health and safety and environmental standards and to a high quality **Requirements** * Degree / Higher Diploma qualification in Construction Management and/ or equivalent trade qualification * Significant experience in a similar position * Excellent knowledge of trades, and in particular carpentry * Background in residential construction * Excellent working knowledge of latest building regulation and certification standards * An ability to achieve demanding time and quality targets **Benefits** * Company vehicle * Competitive salary in one of Ireland's fastest growing PLCs * Comprehensive health insurance for you and your dependents * Contributory pension scheme * An exciting Wellness Programme with events and activities running throughout the year * Access to our Digital Gym * Employee Assistance Programme * Performance related bonus * Regular compensation reviews * Long term illness cover * Peace of mind with life assurance * Paid maternity leave, as well as paternity leave for fathers * If you're cycling, we've got you covered on the cycle\- to\- work scheme Glenveagh is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from suitably qualified candidates from all sections of the community. If you require reasonable accommodation in completing this application, interviewing, or otherwise participating in the recruitment process, please notify the Talent Acquisition team by contacting careers@glenveagh.ie.

title: Fire and Security Technician in the Leinster region
description: We are a nationwide fire and security company with active projects in some of the largest, exciting developments in Ireland. Such as renewable energy projects, large state of the art warehousing projects and large housing complexes. Due to expansion, we are currently recruiting a position which will offer an exciting opportunity to an enthusiastic candidate. The right candidate will find this role varied, interesting and rewarding. This role would suit somebody with a advance knowledge of security systems, past experience essential. The position will provide great experience working with several types of systems on various projects in the Leinster area. This is a great opportunity to commence a long term career in an exciting modern and growing company. Salary will reflect entry level and experience. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €45,000\.00\-€60,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company car * Company pension * Flexitime * Food allowance * Sick pay Work Location: On the road

title: Full Stack Developer
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated 30 years in business and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver technology and transformation solutions that drive customer success. Our deep expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly evolving technology landscape. We foster strong partnerships with global technology leaders including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re an award\-winning employer reflecting how our employees are at the very heart of what we do: * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core values driven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you!. **Job Description** We are seeking a Full Stack Developer to join our team. The successful candidate should have experience across backend and frontend development, including modern JavaScript frameworks and Microsoft technologies. This role involves working on both new and existing systems, contributing to development, testing, and deployment activities. We are looking for someone with analytical problem\-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to collaborate effectively within a team environment while communicating technical concepts clearly. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, build, implement, test, and monitor high\-quality software components for full stack applications * Develop and maintain backend services and APIs * Contribute to frontend development using modern frameworks * Write unit tests to support code quality and reliability * Support CI/CD pipelines for build, deployment, and testing processes * Participate in Agile development practices, including sprint planning, stand\-ups, and retrospectives * Contribute to code reviews and shared coding standards **Qualifications** **Essential Skills \& Experience** * Experience as a Full Stack Developer * Backend development using **.NET Core (C\#)** and **NodeJS/Typescript** * Frontend development using **Angular** or **React**, with **TypeScript** * Experience with **SQL Server** and/or **PostgreSQL** * Understanding of **REST API design and integration** * Experience with **DevOps tools** (e.g. Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, or similar) * Experience supporting **application deployment and CI/CD processes** * Experience developing **scalable features and services** * Problem\-solving skills with a structured and thoughtful approach * Understanding of software development principles and best practices * Experience writing **unit tests** and participating in **code reviews** * Ability to collaborate within cross\-functional teams and communicate technical concepts clearly **Desirable Skills** * Knowledge of **SSIS** and ETL workflows, or interest in developing these skills * Experience with **data migration** activities or exposure to related tools and processes * Experience with **Power BI** and data platforms such as SQL Server or Oracle * Experience working with **AWS services** (e.g. S3, Lambda, Cognito, Amplify) * Experience working with **SSO and SAML** * Experience building products using **LLMs** * Basic prompt engineering knowledge or interest in learning * Interest in developing new systems and projects **Additional Information** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Salary Range: €45,000\-€60,500 * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Version 1 Pension with a 5% employee\-matched contribution, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. * 24 days holiday allowance plus Public Holidays, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& quarterly Star Awards. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and lived experiences, and we value the different perspectives people bring including those shaped by disability and neurodiversity. We want every candidate to have a positive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please contact the recruiter at ekta.bahl@version1\.com. We will consider all requests carefully, respectfully and confidentially. \#LI\-EB1 \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Full Stack Engineer
description: **The Company** The Irish Times Group is Ireland's leading independent quality news publishing organisation. The core activity of The Irish Times Group has evolved from six\-day print publishing to a seven\-day multi\-platform news publisher with a market\-leading position in the development of subscriptions and maximising revenues. We focus on producing quality content for our readers, providing media solutions for customers and we continue to grow and evolve in a fast\-paced changing media landscape. The Company is owned by The Irish Times Trust and is managed by The Irish Times DAC. In recent years, the Irish Times Group expanded its interests to include the Irish Examiner, The Echo, Regional newspapers, in addition to the Citywest print facility, Recruitireland.com, MyHome.ie, RIP.ie, The Gloss and Score Beo. **The Role** We are seeking a Full Stack Developer with mobile and web application experience to join our App Stream team. Working under the guidance of our technical leadership, you'll contribute to building and maintaining the mobile applications and web platforms that serve hundreds of thousands of readers across iOS, Android, and web. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated developer to grow their skills in a high\-impact environment where your work directly influences how audiences engage with Ireland's leading news organisation. You'll work on technical challenges including cross\-platform mobile development, responsive web applications, API integration, and performance optimisation across multiple platforms. **Key Responsibilities;** * Design, develop, and maintain cross\-platform mobile applications using React Native or Flutter * Build and maintain responsive web applications using React * Develop and integrate RESTful APIs and backend services using TypeScript/JavaScript * Implement user interfaces with focus on performance, accessibility, and user experience * Write comprehensive tests and maintain high code quality standards * Participate in code reviews and contribute to technical documentation * Collaborate with Product, Editorial, and Commercial teams to deliver features * Monitor application performance and troubleshoot production issues * Support deployment processes and participate in release management **Requirements \& Experience for the role;** * 3\-5 years of software development experience * Strong TypeScript/JavaScript development skills (backend and frontend) * Proven experience with React for web application development * Experience with cross\-platform mobile development (React Native or Flutter) * Experience with RESTful API design and development * Understanding of database technologies (SQL and NoSQL) * Familiarity with version control (Git) and CI/CD pipelines * Experience working in Agile development environments * Excellent problem\-solving and communication skills * PHP experience desirable **Tech Stack** * Mobile: React Native or Flutter for iOS and Android * Frontend: React, TypeScript/JavaScript, HTML5, CSS3 * Backend: Node.js, TypeScript/JavaScript, potentially PHP * Databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB or similar * Cloud: AWS, Azure, or GCP * DevOps: Git, CI/CD pipelines, Docker * Testing: Jest, React Testing Library, Detox or similar frameworks **Why Now?** We're expanding our App Stream capacity to support growing audience demands and strategic digital initiatives. Your code will power the applications that connect hundreds of thousands of readers to quality journalism daily. This is an opportunity to join a team where your technical skills will have immediate impact on Ireland's leading digital news platforms. The Irish Times Group operate a Hybrid working policy. Our benefits include Health Insurance contribution, Pension Contribution, Sick Leave Cover, Maternity and Paternity Leave, Irish Times Premium Digital Subscription, Income Protection Policy, Corporate Gym Membership discounts, Free Digital GP and Life Assurance Policy.

CRD Engineering organization is innovating and transforming the platform by moving away from monolithic software to utilising next generation cloud\-based technology that leverages Microsoft Azure, Kafka, Snowflake, etc. Industry momentum has seen CRD grow over 135% in headcount over the last 5 years, and we continue to grow. As a digital innovation leader, we invest into our solutions, processes, systems, and talent. We are actively hiring engineers for our Private Market’s division as this is an exciting time to join our innovative engineering teams as we are investing in growing out our European presence and leadership to help drive our next phase of growth. ****Summary:**** Act as Full Stack engineer responsible for the development, enhancement and maintenance of the CRPM modules and components, as part of an agile scrum team, and at times provide troubleshooting assistance to customer support teams. ****Responsibilities:**** * Analyze, design, develop, test, and debug large and complex software enhancements and solutions critical to system operation * Collaborate with engineering team to create simple and sustainable software solutions for complex problems * Direct problem solving for projects or major phases of projects to resolve software technical issues * Provide informed guidance and critical analysis of proposed changes during code reviews * Write unit and automation tests to ensure a high\-quality product * Provide troubleshooting on large, mission critical client implementations * Engage in the agile software development process by adhering to and advancing the CRD scrum methodology, including attending all daily standups, sprint planning, backlog grooming, and retrospectives * Plan and coordinate cross\-team activities to complete assignments ****Qualifications:**** **Recommended Education:** * B.S. degree (or foreign education equivalent) in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, and Physics or other technical course of study required. MS degree strongly preferred. ****Key Skills And Prior Experience:**** * Experience of 3\-5 years developing event driven architectures, financial services a plus * Experience in cloud native development using Java, Spring, Angular or React * Experience in developing observable, operable cloud native software that horizontally scales * Experience with relational databases (RDBMS) * Demonstrated problem solving abilities involving multi\-tiered, high\-performance database applications * Demonstrated ability to design and develop software solutions to business and technical problems of high complexity * Outstanding written and verbal communication skills * Able to work well with peers in a collaborative team environment * Knowledge of Agile development methodology * Employees are expected to work from the office 4 days a week. ****About State Street**** **What we do.** State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. From technology to product innovation we're making our mark on the financial services industry. For more than two centuries, we've been helping our clients safeguard and steward the investments of millions of people. We provide investment servicing, data \& analytics, investments research \& trading and investment management to institutional clients. **Work, Live and Grow.** We make all efforts to create a great work environment. Our benefits packages are competitive and comprehensive. Details vary in locations, but you may expect generous medical care, insurance and savings plans among other perks. You'll have access to flexible Work Program to help match your needs. And our wealth of development programs and educational support will help you reach your full potential. **Inclusion, Diversity, and Social Responsibility.** We truly believe our employees' diverse backgrounds, experience and perspective are a powerful contributor to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and reach their maximum potential while adding value to both our organization and our clients. We warmly welcome the candidates of diverse origin, background, ability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity and personality. Another fundamental value at State Street is active engagement with our communities around the world, both as a partner and a leader. You will have tools to help balance your professional and personal life, paid volunteer days, matching gift program and access to employee networks that help you stay connected to what matters to you. State Street is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. Company: Charles River Development Discover more at www.StateStreet.com/careers ****About State Street**** Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement Job ID: R\-783863

title: Full Transparency - Here's What Happens After You Apply
description: * **Full transparency: from our hiring process to our promotion structure** * **Full meritocracy: 100% merit\-based promotions \+ uncapped commission** * **We value your time: interview with us and receive feedback within 48 hours** **Access** is looking for driven individuals to join us as **Recruitment Consultants** \- no prior experience needed, full training provided \- as we push forward on our mission to become Ireland's \#1 healthcare recruitment agency. We're open to all backgrounds. It's 2026\. Candidates use AI to write CVs. Recruiters use AI to review them. Both ghost each other. We've all been there, and we're done with it. At Access, we do things differently. **Here's exactly what happens after you hit apply:** **The Process:** 1. **You Apply \-** Submit your CV. If there's a fit, you'll hear from me directly: no AI screening, no black hole. I'll give you a quick call to complement what's on your CV and get a feel for your story. If it's a match, your profile goes straight to our hiring managers. 2. **Interview Stage 1 \-** A conversation about your background, your understanding of the role, and your motivation. We're assessing relevant skills and how you think about recruitment. 3. **Interview Stage 2 \-** A short roleplay, live feedback, then another go. We're not grading perfection. We're grading coachability, and grit. 4. **Interview Stage 3 \-** A final chat with one of our directors. We're looking for alignment with Access's values: ownership, accountability, and ambition. 5. **After the Final Stage \-** We move fast. You'll have an answer within 24 hours. Accept the offer, sign the contract, and we get to work. ***Guaranteed: feedback within 48 hours at every interview stage.*** **Compensation:** * Competitive base salary \+ unlimited commission * **Year 1 average total earnings: €45K\+ \| Year 2 average total earnings: €75K\+** * Commission paid on every shift you book: no clawbacks, no limits * Top performers consistently earn well above these figures from day one **What We Look For** * 1\-3 years of proven success in any background (commercial, target\-driven, and fast\-paced environments are very beneficial) * Ambitious, coachable, accountable, and hungry to grow fast * EU citizenship or Stamp 4 visa Ready to start a process you can actually trust? **\+353 (1\) 572 1161 \| cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie**

title: Functional Business Analyst
description: Job Title \- Functional Business Analyst Location \- Dublin, Ireland Mode \- 3 days/week hybrid Type \- Contract Job Description :\- What you will do: * Interacting with business owners, product teams, product vendors, other applications owners, external customers and internal customers to identify requirements and deliver those within stipulated timelines. * Provide content leadership in working with the clients in defining solutions to their business requirements. * Interacting with business stakeholders for requirements gathering, analysis, documentation etc. * Work with the delivery / implementation teams to provide domain inputs and to ensure delivery and implementation of the proposed solution. * 6\-10 years of Industry experience, having worked in Tier\-1 IT organizations in BFSI domain with a focus on Payments Domain experience in Low Value Payments, High Value Payments, Cross Border Payments and key regulations \& associated IT impacts, affecting the Payments market, such as Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA), Instant Payments, Payment Services Directive, Open Banking, CBPR\+, Target Payments, SEPA direct debits etc. * Expertise in Messaging Schemas, and Message formats Required Skillset * Must have in depth solutioning experience in Banking Domain with focus on payments. * Should have the ability to read and understand regulatory changes such as SWIFT / SEPA rulebooks and understand requirements. * Exposure on Instant payments, ISO20022 and PSD2 would be additional benefits. * Well versed in documentation skills such as flow diagrams, procedures and manuals.

title: Fundraising Ambassador - MSF
description: **Looking for more than just a job?** Want to earn well, build confidence, and make a real difference every day? Join **Médecins Sans Frontières (Doctors Without Borders)** as a **Face‑to‑Face Fundraising Ambassador**, representing one of the world’s most trusted humanitarian organisations. You’ll be out in local neighbourhoods, having real conversations, sharing MSF’s life‑saving work, and inspiring people to support medical care where it’s needed most. **This role is NATIONWIDE:** **PLEASE NOTE:** A **full driving licence and access to your own vehicle are essential for this role.** No experience? **No problem.** Full training is provided from day one. **What you’ll be doing:** * Engaging with members of the public in residential areas * Representing MSF’s mission with passion and professionalism * Signing supporters up to long‑term, life‑saving donations * Working as part of a supportive, high‑energy outdoor team **Who this role is perfect for:** * Confident, friendly communicators who enjoy meeting people * Those happy working outdoors and on their feet * Reliable, motivated, and goal‑driven individuals * Caring, community‑minded people who want meaningful work * **Drivers with a full licence and access to a vehicle** **What you’ll get in return:** * **Base salary:** €2,166\.67 – €2,250\.00 **per month (gross)** * **Uncapped commission** – your effort directly increases your earnings * **Fully paid, industry‑leading training \& onboarding** with major Irish charities * Clear progression opportunities, including **leadership \& self‑development training** * **Opportunity to take Fridays off** when weekly targets are reached * Supportive management, coaching, and a strong team culture **Your Working Week:** * **Monday – Friday** * **Variable hours depending on site and campaign** * **Summer Shifts \- 11am \- 7pm OR 12pm \- 8pm** **1‑hour lunch break included**

title: Gelato Maker-part time
description: Gelato maker \-Fully flexible \- Mornings, afternoons, early evenings, weekdays and weekends! We at Gino's Gelato are currently recruiting for the role of Gelato Maker to join our team in our Swords Store. Gino's is a successful country wide chain of Gelato Shops which is experiencing strong growth and we are currently searching for a customer focused individual to join our dynamic team. We are looking to fill the roll of Head Gelato Maker. The successful candidate will need to enjoy customer interaction, deliver excellent customer service, and have a flair for the creative. Previous experience is preferred but not essential as full training will be provided. Ideally candidate will live locally. There is a competitive remuneration package on offer for the right candidate. If you would like to work for a company that values its colleagues and provides a happy work environment, don't hesitate apply today with an up to date CV. Join the Gino's success story, we would love to hear from you. Job Description: * Meet and exceed customer expectation through active customer interaction * Make Gelato fresh every day and produce to company standard and recipes. * Stock control and ordering of product. * Ensure a clean and safe environment is provided at all times for your team and customers alike. * Ensure all company policies are implemented at all times, especially HACCP and Food Safety. If Pride , great quality, and rewarding work appeals to you then apply today to join our team. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Salary: €14\.75

title: General Operative (Morning, Evening & Night shifts)
description: **Your Recipe For a Rewarding Career Starts Here!** **Why Dawn Farms?** We can offer an excellent opportunity to be part of a world\-class team offering permanent, full\-time positions with set hours and plenty of opportunity for further training, development and career opportunities should you wish to progress further. Our culture is one of inclusiveness and diversity with a constant drive and focus for continuous improvement throughout our business. Our production plants are located in Naas, Co Kildare with easy access via public transport. **About Us** Dawn Farms is the largest dedicated supplier of **cooked meat and plant\-based ingredients and fermented products** outside of the US supplying world leading brands across more than 44 markets from our two state of the art plant facilities based in Naas. We also have manufacturing facilities in the UK and Germany. Dawn Farms is one of the largest employers in Naas, Co Kildare with a strong culture of diversity and inclusion. **Job Purpose** Dawn Farms is currently seeking for **General Operatives** to join our **Production Teams.** You will play an integral part of the team in ensuring the highest level of hygiene, food safety and health and safety standards are met. **Positions:** Full\-Time **Location:** Naas, Co. Kildare. **Salary:** €14\.15 \- €18\.39 per hour (overtime opportunities available) **Role Responsibilities \& Duties** * Clean tools, equipment and work surfaces. * Pack and prepare meat. * Dispatch and palletize. * Production line work. * Comply with SOPs. * Adhere to company health and safety policies/procedures. * Adhere to hygiene and food safety measures. **Skills \& Experience** * Be a team player. * Be comfortable working with meat \& in a chiller environment. * Highly motivated and enthusiastic. * Ability to take instruction but also ability to work on own initiative. * Be health and safety conscious. * Flexible to the needs of the business. **What we can offer you?** * Annual leave 171\.60 hours. * Overtime opportunity \& Shift premiums where applicable. * Pension plan. * Health insurance contribtuion plan. * Subsidised canteen. * Employee assistance programme (EAP) \- this programme offers expert wellbeing support 24 hours a day 365 days a year access to counselling, financial information, consumer information, life coaching, health information, autism support, parent coaching, International employee support, all free for you and your family. * Free English classes. * Wellness programme. * Maternity/Paternity leave benefits. * Training \& Development, Career progression. * Christmas Hamper. * Christmas Savings Scheme. * Bike to work scheme.

title: Global Client Executive - Talent Solutions
description: LinkedIn is the world's largest professional network, built to create economic opportunity for every member of the global workforce. Our products help people make powerful connections, discover exciting opportunities, build necessary skills, and gain valuable insights every day. We're also committed to providing transformational opportunities for our own employees by investing in their growth. We aspire to create a culture that's built on trust, care, inclusion, and fun – where everyone can succeed. Join us to transform the way the world works. **Job Description** This role is based Dublin , Ireland At LinkedIn, our approach to flexible work is centered on trust and optimized for culture, connection, clarity, and the evolving needs of our business. The work location of this role is hybrid, meaning it will be performed both from home and from a LinkedIn office on select days, as determined by the business needs of the team. As a LinkedIn Global Client Executive within the EMEA region, you will be the regional lead for our accounts responsible for both renewals and upselling on existing installation combined with a strong focus on developing consistent new business. You will partner closely with the Client Director on your accounts to expand our footprint by aligning LinkedIn’s portfolio of professional solutions (across Talent, Learning and WWU) with the complex requirements of our largest global customers. You will establish yourself as a peer and trusted advisor to senior level economic buyers at each of your clients and develop a deep understanding of their business and the markets in which they operate. You will also invest in and demonstrate an understanding of their strategy and how they execute on same. You will act as the single regional point of contact with our key global customers and conduct executive level commercial discussions with the intention of growing and capturing green, blue and white space. You will be responsible for being the lead on strategic clients with multiple POD language members for cohesion \& best outcome for the business. To ensure our clients generate the highest possible return on their investment, you will ensure that all LinkedIn Products and Solutions are being utilized effectively in the region in collaboration with cross\-functional partners and best practice. Ultimately, you will provide compelling recommendations for additional investments and renewals in LinkedIn. This is a highly collaborative role requiring a passion for building strong and lasting relationships both within LinkedIn and externally with our clients, supporting our largest customers to be, and feel, successful with LinkedIn products and solutions. You will need to lead and support a complex, cross\-functional team to ensure multi\-level engagement with the clients. **Responsibilities:** Success in this role will be your ability to excel in the following: **Client Focus** * Be open, collaborative with XFP and customer\-focused in all communications and transactions with colleagues and clients. * Leverage executive relationships and executive sponsors to deepen LinkedIn’s relationships with clients. * Quickly gain knowledge and expertise of the entire talent suite of LinkedIn Solutions (Talent, Learning \& Glint) to communicate the benefits of new products, features, and enhancements to our clients. * Provide high\-quality customer engagement activities on the solutions they are already invested in. **Sales Excellence** * Partner with the Global Client Director (GCD) of your named accounts to agree upon account goals, strategies and tactics for growth and then build the related account plans. * Own, build and drive the EMEA/LATAM level strategy for your accounts to both contribute to \& align with the global strategy. * Identify key decision makers, regional approach, buying processes, current investment, product utilization and new revenue opportunities. * Prospect to find new business through local entities with zero/low LinkedIn footprint by building weekly pipeline of net new business identification expectation of 1\.5\-2 new opportunities per week. * Negotiate and close all orders with the larger LinkedIn investment in mind and provide post\-contract support to ensure product delivery and satisfaction * Use Dynamics to track activities and, most importantly, accurately forecast your business having CRM always reflect best practice detail. **Leadership** * Coordinate and delegate a cross\-functional team of subject matter experts to support your accounts, drawing on product specialist support when necessary. * Solution sell to our clients to become a part of their tools \& technology ecosystem positioning LinkedIn as a key partner not a vendor. * Maintain ongoing communication with the GCD to keep them updated on new opportunities. * Represent both market trends and client need to the Executive and Product teams to ensure we are both serving current needs well and evolving our portfolio to identify future client needs. * Demonstrate exceptional deal management skills across deals of all levels. * Be active in driving diversity \& LinkedIn culture and Values within the team \& division. **Qualifications** **Basic Qualifications:** * 6\+ years of experience in quota\-carrying roles or equivalent sales consulting experience * 2\+ years in enterprise client's sales * Fluent in English **Preferred Qualifications:** * 1\+ years’ experience selling LinkedIn Learning. * Experience working cross\-functionally to build strategy in EMEA to enhance global deal success. * Success selling to senior talent, learning executives and business partners on the solution of LinkedIn * Proven success in selling brand new, disruptive technology * Client success mindset to maintain legacy revenues and protect long term revenue opportunities. * Experience closing new business accounts and managing existing accounts * Excellent communication and teamwork skills * Demonstrated ability to generate a plan to ensure deep penetration into global accounts and regional entities **Suggested Skills:** * Global Sales * Field/Territory Strategy * Collaboration * Negotiation * Forecasting **Additional Information** **Global Data Privacy Notice and Compliance Posters for Job Candidates** Please use this link to access documents that provide information about how LinkedIn handles the personal data of employees and job applicants, as well as the E\-Verify Participation Notice and the Department of Justice Immigrant and Employee Rights Section Right to Work posters: https://www.linkedin.com/legal/candidate\-portal.

title: Global Trade Compliance Business Analyst
description: **Job Title: Global Trade Compliance Business Analyst** **Duration: 6 months contract with possibility for extensions** **Location: 100% remote for Mumbai, Dublin, Singapore** **Introduction:** We are seeking an experienced Global Trade Compliance Business Analyst to join our team and play **a c** ritical role in supporting the business across a broad spectrum of global import and export compliance matters. This position is vital in ensuring our compliance with international trade regulations while enabling data\-driven decision\-making through the creation of innovative dashboards and reports. The successful candidate will leverage their expertise in global trade compliance and advanced analytics tools to model the potential impact of tariffs on the business model and drive continuous improvement in compliance processes. **Roles and Responsibilities:** * Collaborate with regional trade compliance teams and business stakeholders to gather, document, and refine business requirements and functional processes, adapting them as business needs evolve. * Maintain and oversee day\-to\-day global classification processes to support global import volumes, ensuring accuracy and compliance. * Assist with technology, hardware, and software trade classification, including management of Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS), Schedule B, and Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) classifications. * Manage compliance activities equally focused on imports and exports (50% each). * Develop and contribute to documented classification justifications based on the General Rules of Interpretation (GRI) foundation for global import classifications. * Provide import compliance guidance in adherence to US and international regulations, including conducting research, updating status reports, and coordinating deliverables within the team. * Build, maintain, and analyze data\-informed metrics to generate actionable insights that enhance the global trade compliance program. * Establish and nurture positive business relationships with key stakeholders and cross\-functional teams. * Engage with software development teams to understand and influence evolving compliance technology, identifying opportunities to automate manual processes. * Generate and contribute to performance requirements, timelines, and metrics for all related activities. * Support project management efforts to ensure timely delivery of team assignments and objectives. **Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Business, Supply Chain, Logistics, or a related field. * Minimum of 5 years of experience as a Business Analyst, preferably within supply chain, logistics, or global trade compliance environments. * Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear documentation including Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and detailed reports. * Proven experience with global trade compliance regulations, including import/export laws and logistics preferred. * Demonstrated ability to independently research and dive deep into complex compliance requests and issues. **Tools and Technologies:** * Proficient in Smartsheet, Power BI, and AWS Quicksight with hands\-on experience creating new dashboards, running data batches, and developing meaningful metrics and reports. * Strong data analytics and data entry skills to support compliance reporting and insights generation. * Familiarity with trade classification tools and regulatory databases is a plus.

title: Graduate Engineer
description: **Why Sony Interactive Entertainment?** Sony Interactive Entertainment isn’t just the Best Place to Play — it’s also the Best Place to Work. Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is the company behind the PlayStation brand. As a subsidiary of Sony Group Corporation, we’re part of a proud legacy of innovation and excellence. SIE is a dynamic technology company, delivering cutting\-edge hardware and network services to more than 100 million people and an entertainment leader, home to some of the most beloved and recognizable intellectual properties (IP) in the world. Our role at SIE is to create and nurture the experiences under the PlayStation brand, a name synonymous with entertainment excellence and creativity. **The Role** We are looking for a motivated Engineer to join our team and help create and improve tools in the PlayStation SDK used by game developers worldwide These include tools to manage content, perform firmware updates, load and run game code, carry out remote configuration, capture console, screen and controller logs, remote view consoles and also a distributed resource management system. We are offering a 12\-month fixed\-term contract as part of our wider Graduate Programme within the Product and Technology group. This programme is designed to provide a structured early\-career experience, combining hands\-on work with dedicated development opportunities. As part of the scheme, you will have access to a speaker series featuring leaders and experts from across the organisation, a range of training opportunities to build professional skills, and regular opportunities to connect and network with fellow graduates across the Product and Technology organisation **What You’ll Be Doing** * Analysis of customer requirements to produce feature and or application designs * Provide estimates, design, develop and deliver work to schedule. * Investigate, debug, and resolve issues with both existing and new code features * Participate in requirement and architectural discussions, collaborate with other teams and work in an agile environment. * Attend periodic in\-person workshops or team events (with the possibility of travel) **What We Are Looking For** This is an entry level opportunity. You should bring some of the following skills and experience: * BSc /MSc in Computer Science or equivalent experience * 1\-2 years experience programming in a high level language with a preference towards C\# or C\+\+ * Ability to demonstrate understanding of algorithms, data structures and software architecture * Strong debugging, profiling and performance tuning skills **Highly Desirable Skills** * Experience with UI frameworks and technologies including .NET, Avalonia, WPF and / or MFC * Skilled in using Git, TeamCity, or similar CI/CD workflows * Knowledge of dynamic scripting languages (e.g.Python) Please note, Sony Interactive Entertainment conducts background checks at the offer stage for all new employees (which may include criminal background checks for some roles) and will need to process personal information to support these checks. Please refer to our Candidate Privacy Notice for more information about what personal information we collect, how we use it, who we share it with, and your data protection rights. **Equal Opportunity Statement:** *Sony is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All persons will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender (including gender identity, gender expression and gender reassignment), race (including colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin), religion or belief, marital or civil partnership status, disability, age, sexual orientation, pregnancy, maternity or parental status, trade union membership or membership in any other legally protected category.* *We strive to create an inclusive environment, empower employees and embrace diversity. We encourage everyone to respond.* *Sony Interactive Entertainment is a Fair Chance employer and qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records will be considered for employment.*

title: Graduate Health and Safety Consultant - 2026 Graduate Programme
description: The Opportunity A career at Arup offers you the chance to make a positive difference in the world. Independently owned and independently minded, Arup is the creative force at the heart of many of the world's most prominent projects in the built environment. When you join Arup, you immediately become part of our global community working on some of the world's most recognised projects. Dedicated to sustainable development, Arup is a collective of advisors and experts working across 140 countries. Founded to be both humane and excellent, we collaborate with our clients and partners using imagination, technology and rigour to shape a better world. As a Graduate Health and Safety Consultant at Arup, you will: * Carrying out the duties of Project Supervisor Design Process (PSDP) co\-ordinator on projects of varying sizes and complexities * Carrying out design health and safety reviews with our design teams on projects * Provision of advice to clients and designers in relation to construction, operational and occupational health and safety * Providing assistance to the Company Safety Officers in the implementation of our occupational safety management systems (which is OHSAS 18001 accredited) * Carrying out design and construction safety reviews/inspections on construction sites and operational facilities Does this role inspire you? We're looking for graduates who are currently pursuing a third level qualification in Health and Safety. **We Also Value** * Third level qualification in another technical area within the construction industry, e.g. Engineering, Architecture, Construction Management or similar * Some knowledge of design and construction\-related health and safety legislation * Some understanding of the PSDP role * Some knowledge of Safety Management Systems * Excellent communications, inter\-personal and writing skills * Pro\-active approach to gathering and processing information with good follow\-through to completion * Some construction site experience will also be an advantage Please submit a a copy of your CV, Cover letter and most recent transcripts when applying.

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Danish Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English * Fluency in Danish * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. title: Group Financial Controller
description: **Group Financial Controller** **Location:** On\-site, 5 days per week \- North Dublin **Reports to:** Chief Financial Officer An exciting opportunity has arisen to join a PE\-backed construction group experiencing significant growth. As Group Financial Controller, you will be a key member of the senior finance team, driving financial reporting, controls, and continuous improvement across the organisation. You will lead a large finance team, working closely with the CFO and Group Cost Accountant to support strategic decision\-making and business development. **Responsibilities** * **Financial Reporting** — Lead the preparation and analysis of Group Financial Statements to strict deadlines, including trading updates and commentary papers for stakeholders. * **Forecasting** — Take ownership of financial forecasts for the group and its subsidiary companies. * **Team Leadership** — Manage and develop large accounting teams, providing guidance on technical accounting matters and ensuring ongoing training and professional development. * **Audit \& Compliance** — Oversee the annual external audit engagement, ensuring timely completion and that all statutory requirements are met. * **Financial Controls** — Ensure robust financial controls are in place and consistently applied across the group. * **Continuous Improvement** — Identify and lead projects to enhance finance processes, leveraging technology and systems to improve efficiency, customer service, and the control environment. * **Stakeholder Engagement** — Liaise with key internal and external stakeholders, providing support to business development activities as required. **Requirements** **Essential** * Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) * Minimum 10 years' post\-qualification experience in a senior finance role, ideally within the construction sector * Strong technical accounting expertise * Proven ability to lead and develop teams in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment * Experience managing external audit relationships * Excellent attention to detail and ability to meet tight deadlines **Desirable** * Strong commercial acumen with a track record on commercial projects * Good working knowledge of Irish tax legislation * Experience in complex, multi\-entity group structures * Project management and delivery experience **Personal Attributes** * Excellent communication, influencing, and negotiation skills * Ability to build positive relationships at all levels * Initiative, problem\-solving ability, and a strong record of achievement **Remuneration** * Up to €140,000 plus bonus \& benefits **Contact** * Rachel Tubridy on 086 662 9674 or email rtubridy@peoplesource.ie ***PeopleSource is a finance and accounting recruitment firm.This role will not qualify for sponsorship; therefore all applicants must have the right to work in Ireland.*** ***Due to the level of responses for recently posted roles, similar is expected for this advert also, only successful applicants will be contacted. Apologies in advance but the volume of applications has been too high to be able to contact everyone with feedback. Thanks for understanding.***

title: HR and Data Insights Business Partner- Hybrid
description: **Deloitte is the biggest professional services Firm in the world and making an impact is more than just what we do: it’s why we’re here. We’re driven to create positive progress for our clients, community, people, and the planet. This sense of purpose inspires us to work to the highest standards, to tackle the challenges that matter.** We offer a competitive starting salary of €66,000\.00 for this role, with salary adjustments reflecting your experience level and expertise. . **Why you'll enjoy this new opportunity** The HR Business Partner (‘HRBP’) team supports our Business Units responsible for delivering firm\-wide and departmental strategic HR objectives aligned with key business objectives. This role uniquely combines HR business partnering with advanced data analytics capabilities. The HR and Data Insights Business Partner will support the T\&T (Technology \& Transformation) business and is an integral part of helping to shape our people strategies and initiatives through evidence\-based insights and analytics. You will anticipate business needs, translate complex people data into actionable recommendations, and provide service excellence to key internal stakeholders. This role is extremely varied and involves multiple stakeholder engagement, data analytics projects, and strategic HR initiatives—a fantastic platform to develop experience working within a high\-impact, data\-driven HR model **Key skills and prior experience that align well with this position** * Experience in Human Resources with generalist and Business Partnering experience desirable Proven expertise in HR data analytics, with demonstrated ability to design, build, and interpret complex HR dashboards and reports * Ability to quickly build trust with leaders to influence and challenge and have difficult conversations constructively and confidently * Confident in building strong relationships with internal client groups and HR colleagues * Excellent analytical skills and attention to detail, with proven ability to analyse and interpret HR data to drive strategic decisions and identify trends * Advanced proficiency in MS Excel (including pivot tables, VLOOKUP, data modelling) and visual analytics tools such as Power BI and Tableau * Ability to advise on a range of HR topics including performance management, talent management, employee relations and employee engagement, underpinned by data insights * Good knowledge of leading trends in the field of Human Resources, including HRIS, people analytics, and advanced reporting methodologies. * Excellent communication skills, both written and oral, with ability to translate technical data insights for non\-technical audiences * Demonstrated ability to work on own initiative * Solid understanding of employee lifecycle processes and HR data governance **What type of work will I be doing?** You will be working on business activities which include: * Partnering with the business to deliver a first\-class HR service which is business led and enabled by people to drive our people and purpose ambition * Managing firm wide programs to deliver our talent agenda for T\&T with Centre of Excellence support in areas such as performance management, learning and development, annual reward and recognition, graduate recruitment, experienced hire recruitment, employee engagement, diversity \& inclusion initiatives etc * Reporting and Analytics : design, develop, and maintain advanced HR analytics dashboards and reports; conduct deep\-dive data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities; provide high\-quality, timely reporting and advanced analytics, transforming complex people data into clear, actionable insights that inform strategic decision\-making and enable the delivery of key business objectives. * This includes workforce planning analytics, attrition trends, talent pipeline analysis, engagement metrics, and performance management insights. HR Projects: provide support to the HRBPs with people and talent initiatives and projects as they arise **Who is the Hiring Manager?** The hiring manager is an experienced Director in our People and Purpose team. They prioritise open communication, professional growth, and cultivating a positive team environment. Their leadership style combines high expectations with empathy, promoting innovation and ongoing learning, while maintaining a strong commitment to client service excellence and operational integrity. **Where is this role based?** * Dublin, Hybrid role, Deloitte Works webpage *If you believe that you meet most of the criteria above, we encourage you to apply. Deloitte is an equal opportunities employer and will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process.* **What We Offer** *Your reward at Deloitte is competitive, supports our purpose and enables our people to never stop growing. Your reward at Deloitte is not just your salary, we believe in investing in our people's personal and professional development and empowering their work\-life balance. Our ambition is to achieve gender equality and enable continuous growth. We offer supports and benefits that suit you, wherever you are in life, including health and well\-being, pension, savings, training, coaching and enhanced leave options among others.* *Keep on being you. Bring your full, absolute self to work, every day. Explore, question and collaborate. Stretch your thinking, while building a career that inspires and energises you. And, whatever motivates you, keep growing both professionally and personally – because when you make an impact that matters, we do too.*

title: Hebrew or Danish Customer Support
description: Hebrew or Danish Speaking Technical Customer Support Specialist 📍 Sandyford, Dublin 18(Hebrew), Dublin 4(Danish) (Onsite) 🕒 Full\-Time \| 40 Hours per Week 📅 Rotational Schedule \| Monday–Sunday between 8am–10pm (No Night Shifts) Are you a Hebrew or Danish speaker with strong technical problem\-solving skills and a passion for delivering excellent customer experiences? Join a global leader in digital innovation and support one of the world’s most recognised online advertising platforms. This is an exciting opportunity to build expertise in cutting\-edge digital products while working in a fast\-paced, international environment with strong career growth potential. What You’ll Be Doing * Become a product expert across a complex digital advertising platform * Investigate and resolve technical issues affecting advertisers and internal sales teams * Manage cases end\-to\-end, ensuring timely resolution and excellent service delivery * Identify whether issues are caused by product bugs, user confusion, or technical errors * Work closely with specialist support teams and engineers on escalated cases * Clearly explain solutions, workarounds, and next steps to stakeholders * Help improve the customer experience by providing high\-quality technical support * Support team development through mentoring and knowledge sharing What We’re Looking For * Native or fluent Hebrew or Danish with strong English communication skills * Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working with digital tools, systems, and online platforms * Experience in customer support, technical support, or online operations is highly desirable * Experience working in KPI or target\-driven environments * Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify technical issues * Positive team player with the ability to adapt quickly to new systems and products Why Join? * Join a globally recognised organisation with excellent training and development * Build valuable experience in tech, digital advertising, and platform operations * Clear progression opportunities in operations, support, and specialist teams * Inclusive, collaborative, and multicultural workplace * No night shifts Important Information * Sponsorship is not available for this role * Applicants must already hold full valid working rights in Ireland If you're a Hebrew or Danish speaker looking to grow your career in tech support within a world\-class environment, this could be a great next move.
