title: Operational Analyst
description: * Hybrid **Job Description** AmTrust is a global insurance provider offering risk\-management solutions and business underwriting tailored to the unique needs of each client. AmTrust International underwrites over $2\.5bn of gross written premiums and employs more than 1500 people in 14 countries. We’re looking for a detail\-oriented and analytical Operational Analyst to join our Due Diligence team. This role plays a key part in supporting the onboarding, monitoring, and governance of third\-party partners, ensuring all due diligence processes are executed efficiently and in line with regulatory requirements. You will work closely with teams across Underwriting, Compliance, Risk, and Operations, combining strong data analysis with regulatory awareness and stakeholder engagement. Key Responsibilities * Conduct end\-to\-end due diligence reviews on third parties, including onboarding and ongoing monitoring * Analyse data to identify risk indicators, trends, and potential exposures * Ensure compliance with FCA regulations, governance frameworks, and internal policies * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date records across internal systems and due diligence databases * Produce high\-quality reports to support governance committees and business decision\-making * Act as a point of contact for internal stakeholders and external partners, managing queries through to resolution * Identify and escalate risks or inconsistencies in documentation or submissions * Contribute to the continuous improvement of due diligence processes and controls We’re looking for candidates with experience in insurance, financial services, or another regulated environment, who bring a strong understanding of risk, compliance, or due diligence processes. You should have advanced analytical skills, with proficiency in Excel, and the ability to interpret complex data with a high level of accuracy and attention to detail. Strong stakeholder management and communication skills are essential, alongside the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet tight deadlines. Knowledge of FCA regulations, delegated authority, or third\-party oversight would be beneficial. We’re particularly interested in individuals with a proactive and curious mindset, who demonstrate strong risk awareness and can balance detailed analysis with a broader commercial perspective. You’ll be a collaborative team player who thrives in a fast\-paced, evolving environment, and is motivated by continuous improvement, learning, and professional development Click

title: Options Sales Middle Office Analyst
description: **Overview** Joining the Client team as a Sales Operations Analyst supporting the Options Sales business, you will be responsible for Susquehanna’s options sales desk’s post trade processes to provide a seamless client experience. This role involves working closely with our sales traders, traders, and counterparties. Due to the high volume of trades, we are looking for candidates who have exceptional attention to detail. This role will offer a great exposure to a front office Sales \& Trading environment and will be an excellent opportunity if you have strong communication skills and an interest in financial markets. Being directly exposed to client interactions, a good level of commercial awareness is desired. **In This Role You Will** * Responsible for post trade processes * Execution and allocation of orders through internal/external systems * Act as a first point of contact for any issues related to crossing and settling trades across multiple exchanges, countries and clients * Address each client’s specific post trade requirements and tailor the process to their needs. For example, clients trading on CFD or making sure trades with FTT are accounted for correctly. * Identify opportunities to improve the overall process, automate tools where possible in order to enhance the broader desk’s performance and efficiency. This could include working with the static data team to improve our flex trading process and working with app support and developers to improve the desks tools * Liaise with other teams to develop and work on ad hoc projects aimed at improving the team’s workflow **What We're Looking For** * Minimum of a Bachelors’ degree * Ability to prioritise and complete multiple tasks in a fast paced, dynamic environment * High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. Python a plus * Strong communicator (verbal and written) * Ability to work under pressure and multi\-task * Ambitious and self\-motivated, not afraid to ask questions and use own initiative * Effective problem solving and analytical skills; including effective troubleshooting in a fast\-paced, time\-critical environment * Experience in financial markets is not required although we welcome those with experience * Excellent attention to detail **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Order Fulfillment Exec
description: **Role Overview** The Order Fulfilment Executive is responsible for ensuring that all customer orders are processed, delivered, and invoiced efficiently and accurately. This role is critical to maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction and requires close collaboration with internal teams including Sales, Sales Support, Product, and Finance. **Key Responsibilities** * Accurately process customer sales orders, ensuring all details are reviewed and verified * Manage order processing through CRM and internal systems * Monitor open orders, track progress, and provide timely updates to account managers and customers * Coordinate the delivery and installation of services for customers * Identify and resolve any issues related to order fulfilment or service delivery * Provide administrative support across the business when required * Manage and respond to requests within a centralised inbox **Skills \& Experience** **Essential Requirements** * Understanding of order fulfilment processes and customer service best practices * Previous experience in order fulfilment, customer service, or a sales support/administrative environment * Experience using CRM systems and Microsoft Office tools * Strong data entry and analytical capabilities * Excellent organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines * High level of accuracy and attention to detail **Behavioural Competencies** * Positive, proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and support colleagues and customers * Strong team player, comfortable working in a collaborative environment * Adaptable and open to change in processes, systems, and priorities * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced, evolving environment **Experience** * 2–3 years’ experience in a procurement, sales support, or related operational role **Reporting Line** * Reports to: Head of Sales Support * Direct line management: Sales Support Manager

title: Overnight Resident Experience Associate
description: **Role Summary** We are seeking a motivated Overnight Resident Experience Associate to join our team at Alta Verde, a premier Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) property located in Cross Avenue, Blackrock, Dublin. This is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to progress their career in the growing BTR industry, working for a company that is expanding its footprint in Ireland. The successful candidate, reporting to the Property Manager, will be integral to daily property operations, focusing on resident relations, health and safety, cleanliness and security. This highly visible role will involve regular interaction with residents and visitors helping ensure high standards in resident engagement and satisfaction. We’re seeking candidates with strong experience in customer engagement. This job would ideally suit candidates from hospitality roles like: Security Operative, Night Auditor, Assistant Night Manager / Night Manager. CompassRock has a dedicated and comprehensive training and certification program, and this role will have the potential to progress into more managerial role over time. Some of the training programs include The Property Institute’s Build to Rent course and Managing Safely Qualifications. **Company Overview** CompassRock provides asset and property management services to the rental housing sector. Originally founded in the U.S., the company has expanded to the UK and is rapidly growing its portfolio of Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) assets across England and Europe. CompassRock is in an exciting growth phase, actively acquiring and developing new assets at a fast pace. With an entrepreneurial and innovative culture embedded in its DNA, the company values the dynamic, forward\-thinking individuals it employs. As CompassRock continues to expand, there will be abundant opportunities for career advancement and professional development. **Key Responsibilities** **Customer Service \& Resident Relations:** * Policy and Standards Adherence: Uphold the company’s customer service standards to deliver high\-quality service and resident satisfaction. * Issue Resolution: Address resident complaints and actively troubleshoot problems to take ownership in challenging situations, escalating issues to the Property Manager when necessary. * Resident Engagement Initiatives: Support the planning and execution of community\-building activities that foster positive resident relations and engagement. * Resident Support: Provide residents with appropriate information throughout the tenancy lifespan, including providing renewal information, ensuring legal requirements are met and answering any questions that might occur during the tenancy lifespan. * Relationship Building: Cultivate strong relationships with residents, ensuring professional and prompt communication. Regularly engage with residents and prospective tenants to build strong rapport and trust. **Leasing Support:** Provide administrative support to the Leasing team, as required. * Enquiries: Greet and assist prospective tenants and to qualify and register enquiries and to book viewing appointments at our properties (if required). * Viewings: Conduct informative and engaging viewings to prospective tenants being well\-equipped to answer any questions regarding the property (if required). * Administrative Support: Working with the leasing team to help maintain accurate and organised records of leases, applications, and tenant correspondence. **Operational Activities** * Property Support: Contribute to maintaining a safe and attractive property for residents. Facilities Management Support: Assist the Property Manager with managing projects, overseeing outsourced services for building maintenance, and ensuring timely, high\-quality completion of tasks. * Cleanliness: Complete overnight cleaning of the amenity spaces, ensuring that the property is “resident ready” each day. * ‘Stock’ Coordination: Collaborate with the property manager and procurement team to ensure adequate stock of goods and services for smooth property operations. Completing stock checks on a monthly basis. * Inspections: Conduct / support inventories, move\-in and move\-out inspections, documenting property conditions. * Administrative Support: Assist the Property Manager with all other general administrative duties, as required. **Targets And KPIs** * Work diligently to meet specified targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) set by the management team. **Personal Qualities** * Attention to Detail: Demonstrated focus on quality and compliance in previous work or education (desired). * Proactive and Initiative\-Driven: Able to foresee potential issues and address them before they escalate (desired). Sharing suggestions on how to improve efficiencies, encourage proactivity and support your colleagues. * Flexibility: Ability to adapt to changing circumstances, particularly in a fast\-paced property environment (desired). * Positive, 'can\-do' Attitude: A willingness to learn from, and provide support to, other team members, while delivering top levels of service to all residents (essential). **Skills** * Communication Skills: Both written and verbal, for interacting with residents, staff, and external stakeholders (essential) * Problem\-Solving: Strong abilities in addressing resident disputes (essential). * Customer Service: Demonstrated experience in delivering exceptional customer service (essential). * Computer Literacy: While training on our property management systems will be provided, we require that the applicant be proficient with Microsoft Office and Outlook and have an aptitude for learning new software (essential). **Qualifications (desired)** * Bachelor’s degree in business administration, or a related field (desired) * Full clean Irish driver’s licence **Remuneration/ Bonuses / Benefits** * Base salary:€37,400 * Bonus: Discretionary Bonus Scheme * Benefits: Private health insurance; Pension Scheme; Cycle to Work Scheme; Company Sick Pay. **Working Hours** * 5\-day working week (40 hours) including weekends (Saturday and Sunday). * This role includes nighttime shift work (between 22:30 – 07:30\) **Location:** This is a client facing role and must be based on site at the rostered location(primarily at Alta Verde, Cross Avenue, Blackrock, Dublin. but may be required to work at other locations as required by the business). **Equal Opportunities Statement** We are an Equal Opportunities Employer, committed to creating an inclusive, diverse, and equitable workplace. We provide equal opportunities for everyone and offer reasonable adjustments to ensure all candidates have a fair chance during the recruitment process.

title: PC Specialist
description: **Company Overview** Perry Ellis International, Inc. represents a diverse portfolio of lifestyle apparel brands. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries owns and licenses nationally and with a brand portfolio of over 40 Brands domestically and internationally recognized brands including: Perry Ellis®, Original Penguin® by Munsingwear®, Laundry by Shelli Segal®, Rafaella®, Cubavera®, Ben Hogan®, Savane®, Grand Slam®, John Henry®, Manhattan®, Axist® and Farah®. The Company enhances its roster of brands by licensing trademarks from third parties, including: Nike® for swimwear, and Callaway®, PGA TOUR®, and Jack Nicklaus® for golf apparel. **Position Summary** As the PC Specialist, you will serve as a vital, hands\-on technical anchor on\-site at our Dublin, GA distribution center, reporting directly to the Client Systems Support Manager. In this highly active role, you will walk the warehouse floor to provide critical Tier 1 hardware and software installation, maintenance, and rapid break\-fix repair to keep our high\-volume logistics operations running seamlessly. Your time will be dynamically split between maintaining specialized warehouse equipment—specifically Zebra label printers and RFID handheld scanners (60%)—and supporting desktop PCs, connectivity, and telephony systems (40%). By managing the daily incident ticketing queue each morning, collaborating with System Administrators on facility upgrades, and maintaining meticulous asset and e\-waste records, you will directly prevent operational downtime. To succeed, you must be a highly reliable, quick\-learning team player who remains resilient under pressure, possesses the physical ability to lift up to 60 pounds, and can comfortably commit to a rotating 4\-day, 10\-hour shift schedule (7:00 AM – 5:45 PM) that includes working weekends twice a month. **Key Responsibilities** * Warehouse \& Logistics Hardware Support: Maintain, troubleshoot, and repair critical logistics equipment. This role is highly active, with 60% of your time dedicated to Zebra label printers and RFID handheld scanners, and 40% focused on desktop PCs and connectivity. * On\-the\-Floor Troubleshooting: Actively walk the warehouse floor to provide immediate, face\-to\-face technical assistance to personnel, ensuring proper operational efficiency of all systems. * Installation \& Asset Maintenance: Install, de\-install, configure, and upgrade computers, software applications, and peripheral equipment. Maintain an accurate inventory of computer hardware, parts, and maintenance/repair records, while preparing legacy equipment for proper e\-waste recycling. * Daily Incident Management: Monitor and manage the local ticketing queue first thing every morning, prioritizing and resolving critical hardware and software incidents ASAP to prevent operational downtime. * System \& Facility Collaboration: Perform routine account unlocks and basic troubleshooting workflows using internal platforms like WMOS. Coordinate occasionally with external vendors, the Telecomm team to support telephone equipment, and System Administrators to assist with server equipment changes. * Operational Availability: Work seamlessly within a 10\-hour shift framework (7:00 AM – 5:45 PM) on a rotating 4\-day work\-week schedule, which requires working weekends approximately twice a month. **Qualifications** * Technical Support Experience (Must\-Have): 1–2\+ years of proven, on\-premise technical support experience focusing on hardware/software installation, troubleshooting, and break\-fix workflows. * Specialized Hardware Expertise (Must\-Have): Direct, hands\-on experience troubleshooting, configuring, and repairing Zebra Label Printers and RFID handheld scanners. * Operational Availability (Must\-Have): Absolute reliability and flexibility to work the required 10\-hour shifts (7:00 AM – 5:45 PM) on a rotating schedule, including required weekend shifts (2x per month). * Physical Mobility \& Lifting (Must\-Have): Ability to move around easily, walk frequently across an expansive warehouse environment, and safely lift or move computer/peripheral equipment weighing up to 60 pounds. * Soft Skills (Must\-Have): Exceptional customer service orientation, strong multitasking abilities, and crisp people/communication skills. * Technical Qualifications (Strong Plus): An Associate Degree in a technical field or active certifications such as CompTIA A\+ and/or MCDST. * Ticketing \& Systems Knowledge (Strong Plus): Experience utilizing enterprise ticketing systems (e.g., ServiceNow, Zendesk, Remedy, SolarWinds) and hands\-on familiarity supporting Dell computer systems. * Language Skills (Strong Plus): Bilingual fluency in English and Spanish to effectively communicate with a diverse operations workforce. **Personal Attributes** * Highly Reliable \& Disciplined: Exceptionally dependable with a strong commitment to strict schedule adherence, consistent attendance, and supporting the broader team. * Quick Learner: Able to rapidly absorb new technical workflows, system layouts, and specialized warehouse software under minimal supervision. * Resilient Under Pressure: Calm, level\-headed, and efficient when troubleshooting critical hardware failures during high\-volume operational windows in a fast\-paced environment. * Detail\-Oriented \& Organized: Thrives when multitasking across varied technical tasks, from active physical repairs to maintaining pristine documentation and asset records. **What’s In It For You?** * Comprehensive Health: Premium Medical, Dental, and Vision plans designed to keep you and your family healthy and thriving. * Financial Security: Secure your future with our 401(k) retirement plan (including a discretionary company match) plus company\-provided Basic Life and AD\&D Insurance. * Work\-Life Balance: Take the time you need to recharge with generous Paid Time Off (PTO) and a full schedule of paid Holidays. * Growth \& Support: We invest in your potential through our Tuition Reimbursement program for continuous learning and provide 24/7 personal support via our Member Assistance Program (MAP). * Team Culture: Join a community that celebrates your hard work with regular Associate Engagement Events, ranging from seasonal celebrations to on\-site wellness activities. * Exclusive Style Perks: Look your best for less with generous Associate Clothing Discounts across all our company stores and e\-commerce sites. **Legal Disclaimer** *Perry Ellis International is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their individual background and characteristics. We are committed to providing a workplace free of any discrimination or harassment. This commitment extends to all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, transfer, compensation, and termination. By submitting your application, you acknowledge and agree to the company's commitment to equal employment opportunities.* *We encourage you to read and understand our Privacy Policy here* *.*

title: PE and SPHE Teacher
company: Kilkenny and Carlow Education and Training Board - Training Services
description: Apply for job **Job reference:** 000403 **Closing date:** 26/06/2026 **Location:** Saplings Carlow Special School **Employment type:** Fixed Term **Hours Per Week:** 9 (SPHE 5 hours and PE 4 hours per week) Where to find us *location\_on* Get directions **Job Description** **Teaching and learning is the core focus of Kilkenny and Carlow ETB and we are committed to recruiting high quality teachers with appropriate qualifications, expertise and experience.** ****Qualifications**** * It is an advantage if the candidate has a post graduate qualification in Special Education Needs/Learning Support or be in the process of applying for or completing this qualification. * Applicants must meet the general and special requirements as set out from time to time by the Teaching Council of Ireland for the advertised subject(s). Further details and a full list of recognised qualifications are available on www.teachingcouncil.ie. * Applicants who are in their final year of degree/postgraduate diploma which will lead to a teaching qualification, but who have not yet completed their final exams and/or received their results, may apply for advertised vacancies, subject to the provisions regarding Teaching Council registration set out below. Any offer of interview or employment is made pending the applicant’s results meeting the requirements of the vacancy. ****Registration With The Teaching Council Of Ireland (Essential)**** * Applicants must be currently registered with the Teaching Council of Ireland. * Applicants shall provide a current Registration Certificate, or confirmation of Registration with their Application Form for a teaching position. **Teaching** **Competences** Persons Appointed To Teaching Positions Within Kilkenny And Carlow ETB Will Be Required To Show Evidence Of The Following Key Competences: * + Professional Knowledge + Professional Practice + Communication and relationship building + Self\-awareness and self\-management skills Candidates will be required to describe in no more than 300 words per competency some of their personal achievements to\-date that demonstrate the key competencies required for the position as outlined. Candidates should describe situation/s from their own experience, which they think are the best example/s of what they have done which demonstrates the specific competencies. The example(s) may be drawn from candidates experience in various settings including their professional, community or voluntary involvement. **Professional Knowledge** *Demonstrate in\-depth knowledge, comprehension and understanding of both the subject discipline and pedagogy* ***Indicators Include:*** * Has a strong knowledge of subject matter. * Has an awareness and understanding of specific requirements of course/syllabus/specifications. * Keeps up to date with subject matter/course syllabus changes and developments in the education system. * Understands the cross\-curricular links to other content areas and is able to integrate learning across and between content areas (e.g. literacy, numeracy, critical thinking). * Knows, understands and critically evaluates the range of teaching and learning philosophies / theories and knows how to apply them appropriately within their subject (s). * Is aware of the range of supports available to support curriculum development and implementation. * Participates in and contributes to educational planning and development within the school e.g. lesson planning, subject department planning, etc. **Professional Practice** *Understands the nature of high quality teaching and learning and demonstrates the skills and competencies to illustrate these.* ***Indicators Include:*** ***Planning and Preparation*** * Plans and communicates clear, challenging, appropriate and achievable expectations for students. * Shows knowledge and understanding of how to structure and schedule schemes of work, class plans, subject department plans etc. * Knows and understands the relationship between the planning, implementation and evaluation of the curriculum. * Selects, creates and pitches class materials which respond to the needs of students. **Teaching and Learning** * Understands the factors that contribute to a stimulating learning environment and the role of the teacher in supporting this environment. * Understands the practices that underpin effective classroom teaching and meet the learning styles of a diverse range of students e.g. differentiation, learning intentions, group work, etc. * Establishes standards of behaviour that enable all students to learn and deals with inappropriate behaviour in the context of school policies. * Demonstrates in\-depth knowledge and understanding of classroom management techniques and skills. * Effectively manages the range of behaviours/situations that can occur in the classroom in line with School Code of Behaviour maintaining good order and discipline among students. * Effectively uses a variety of teaching materials and technologies to actively engage students in the learning process e.g. collaboration, technology enhanced teaching and learning. ***Assessment*** * Focuses on assessment for learning by monitoring student progress, giving constructive feedback and helping students to reflect on and improve their learning e.g. formative and summative assessment. * Selects from a range of assessment strategies to evaluate student learning, and uses this information in their planning to help make their teaching more effective. * Assesses the levels of student attainment against relevant benchmarking data and understands the relationship between student assessment and target setting. * Liaises orally and in written reports in an effective manner with parents/guardians on student progress and achievement. **Communication and relationship building** *Establishes effective communication and engagement to ensure positive and effective working relationships with management, colleagues, students, parents and the wider school community.* ***Indicators Include:*** * Seeks to foster positive relationships with students, colleagues, parents, school management and the wider school community in a professional, collaborative, supportive and respectful manner. * Communicates and contributes effectively and collaboratively within the school team environment. * Shows the capacity and skills to relate to and communicate effectively with students, parents, colleagues, school management and the wider school community. * Supports conflict resolution in all aspects of teaching role. * Motivates, inspires and celebrates student effort and success. * Is committed to equality and inclusion and to respecting and accommodating diversity. * Understands how to exercise a duty of care to students. * Participates in and supports the extra\-curricular programmes/activities of the school. * Contributes positively to the school community. ****Self\-awareness And Self\-management Skills**** *Is self\-aware and has the capacity to self\-manage and develop personally and professionally* . ***Indicators Include:*** * Engages in reflective practice of their own work and consistently reviews it in order to ensure it is of the highest possible standard and responsive to changing teaching demands. * Maintains a strong focus on personal and professional growth including continual professional development, participation in professional learning communities, group reflection and practice. * Has the confidence, resilience and optimism to maintain an emotional balance in challenging situations and the capacity to work through these situations. * Is open and responsive to constructive feedback regarding their professional practice and, if necessary, seeks appropriate support, advice and guidance. * Is aware of the Teaching Council Code of Professional Conduct and Social Media Guidelines and is familiar with its professional requirements. * Practices within the statutory framework and regulations pertaining to education, including child protection and health and safety guidelines. * Upholds professional integrity for example discretion, confidentiality, loyalty and trust, empathy etc in all aspects of their professional role as a teacher. * Is aware of the broader student context and their requirement to model best practice in all aspect of their role and relationships in the school community. Please check your email (including spam folders) for updates. If you have any technical issues when applying for this position, please contact Jobtrain at; https://jobseekersupport.jobtrain.co.uk/support/home where you can log a ticket or call 0044 161 850 2004\. FAQ's are available in the top right menu once you have registered. (Please note Jobtrain hours are from 9am to 5\.30pm, and queries may take up to 24hrs to be answered). **Take a look around the organisation** https://kcetb.ie/

title: Part-Time Front of House/Receptionist
description: **The Role / Location** The overall purpose of this role will be to manage the Reception and Front of House on a daily basis and provide a five\-star service to the corporate clients. This is a high\-profile reception role and requires the highest standard of corporate presentation and communication. This is a part\-time, 4 days per week role. **Key Responsibilities** Key responsibilities of the role include but are not limited to: * Carrying out all Reception and Front of House duties as part of the Front Office Team * Meeting and greeting visitors in a professional and welcoming manner, promoting a positive image at all times * Managing the switchboard and directing all calls efficiently, promptly, and professionally * Providing assistance and support to all visitors as required * Monitoring security cameras and promptly notifying the Facilities Manager of any security concerns or breaches * Managing and distributing incoming and outgoing post * Maintaining the reception area to a consistently high standard, ensuring it is clean, tidy, and well organised * Signing in and coordinating access for maintenance contractors in line with site procedures * Ensuring complete client satisfaction through the prompt and efficient handling of queries in a friendly and professional manner * Anticipating client and tenant needs and delivering service that meets and exceeds expectations **Person Specification** The successful candidate will: 1. Be highly customer\-focused, flexible, and extremely well organised 2. Enjoy working in a corporate, front of house environment 3. Demonstrate excellent communication and interpersonal skills 4. Present to a high standard in a corporate setting and have strong client relationship skills 5. Have the ability to multi\-task effectively while demonstrating excellent attention to detail **Key Competencies** 1. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills 2. Immaculate professional presentation 3. Previous experience in a Front of House or corporate reception role 4. A positive “can\-do” attitude, with the confidence to interact with a wide range of stakeholders **Savills Ireland is an equal opportunity employer.** *Savills aims to attract, recruit and retain the best people from the widest possible pool of talent. As such, we are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse working environment for all employees by striving at all times, to be an organisation which does not discriminate, values everyone’s talents and abilities and where diversity is positively promoted.*

title: Patient Feedback and Support Coordinator  – special projects
description: **Purpose of role** The purpose of this post is to work with the Patient Feedback and Experience Lead, Quality and Patient Safety team, QPS General Manager, and Director of Quality on all relevant activities in the Patient Feedback and Support Service related to the management of QPS special projects, internal and external reviews and enquiries, and integration with the HSE. **Essential Criteria** * A third level qualification (minimum Level 8 on QQI framework) in healthcare, quality, patient safety, patient feedback or related field * A minimum of 3\-5 years’ relevant experience in a healthcare setting * Good working knowledge of complaint management processes and relevant legislation (e.g. Health Act 2004, Your Service Your Say) * Excellent ICT skills * Excellent written and oral communication skills, including report writing and presentation skills * Able to work across a wide portfolio with multiple concurrent projects and tasks to be prioritised and completed within required timeframes **How To Apply And Informal Enquiries** Applications for this post must be accompanied by a cover letter, setting out relevant experience that illustrates how the essential criteria listed above is met. The criterion for short listing is based on the requirements of the post, as outlined in the eligibility criteria. * Please note that you must submit a cover letter with your CV, this forms part of your application and CV’s will not be accepted without a detailed cover letter. The closing date for submissions of CV’s and cover letter is **6th July 2026 by5 pm.** Applications must be completed through the advertised post on **CHI.jobs** by clicking **‘Apply for Job’.** **Applications will not be accepted through direct email or any other method.** For informal enquiries for this specialty/department, please contact Kevin O’Farrell, Patient Experience and Feedback Lead, Kevin.OFarrell@childrenshealthireland.ie / (01\) 409 6715\. For other queries relating to this recruitment process, please contact Talent Acquisition Specialist – Taurai machuwe at taurai.machuwe@childrenshealthireland.ie **PLEASE NOTE:** CHI has transitioned to a process of a one commencement day per month for all new employees, CHI internal transfers and Secondments. This update to our Onboarding process is aligned to changes in our monthly/fortnightly payroll and with the launch of our new corporate induction program. title: Personal Lines Administrator
description: **Introduction** At Gallagher, we help clients face risk with confidence because we believe that when businesses are protected, they’re free to grow, lead, and innovate. You’ll be backed by our digital ecosystem: a client\-centric suite of consulting tools making it easier for you to meet your clients where they want to be met. Advanced data and analytics providing a comprehensive overview of the risk landscape is at your fingertips. Here, you’re not just improving clients' risk profiles, you’re building trust. You’ll find a culture grounded in teamwork, guided by integrity, and fueled by a shared commitment to do the right thing. We value curiosity, celebrate new ideas, and empower you to take ownership of your career while making a meaningful impact for the businesses we serve. If you’re ready to bring your unique perspective to a place where your work truly matters; think of Gallagher. **Overview** Gallagher Ireland, part of the globally renowned Arthur J. Gallagher Group, is seeking a dedicated and detail\-oriented Personal Lines Administrator to join our team. This is a full\-time, permanent position based in Dublin. With over 100 years of combined insurance experience, Gallagher Ireland offers tailored business, personal, and financial insurance solutions to protect what matters most to our customers. As part of a global organisation with over 56,000 employees and a presence in more than 130 countries, you’ll be joining a team that values innovation, service excellence, and a commitment to professional growth. **How You'll Make An Impact** In this role, you’ll play a vital part in supporting our Personal Lines team by ensuring the smooth administration of insurance policies and providing exceptional service to our clients. Your responsibilities will include: * Providing general administrative support to the Personal Lines team. * Assisting in the preparation, issuance, and processing of personal lines insurance policies, endorsements, and renewals. * Maintaining and updating client records in the company database, ensuring accuracy and confidentiality. * Responding to client inquiries via phone, email, or in person, providing information on policy details, claims, and billing. * Coordinating with providers to obtain quotes, process endorsements, and resolve discrepancies. * Supporting the team in preparing reports, presentations, and documentation for client meetings. * Monitoring and following up on outstanding client requirements to ensure timely task completion. * Assisting in the claims process by gathering necessary documentation and liaising with relevant contacts. * Ensuring compliance with company policies, industry regulations, and legal requirements. * Participating in team meetings and contributing to process improvement initiatives. **About You** Here’s what you’ll bring to the role: * Proven experience in a busy office administration role, with excellent communication and organisational skills. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (essential). * Experience using Applied software (advantageous). * Strong time management skills and the ability to multi\-task in a fast\-paced, customer\-focused environment. * A self\-motivated, enthusiastic, and team\-oriented approach with a keen eye for detail.

title: Pharmacovigilance Officer (Hybrid)
description: TCP Homecare is a healthcare service provider, specialising in 'direct to patient services' which include the dispensing and distribution of pharmaceutical products, home care nursing services and sharps waste management, facilitating a unique turnkey solution for the delivery of hospital care in the patient's home. We are looking for pharmacovigilance officerswho demonstrates a high degree of self\-motivation and the ability to adapt to change. We have both permanent and contract roles available. **This position is ideally suited to a recent graduate looking to gain experience in pharmacovigilance.** Key Responsibilities: * Process PV events, and product quality complaints in line the organisations operating procedures * Perform mailbox triage and management, case processing, reconciliation, query management and other tasks to ensure compliance with pharmacovigilance procedures. * Be involved in carrying out regular reconciliation of case reports with internal and external sources. * Ensure compliance with internal TCP SOPS and other relevant procedures where applicable * Maintain the Pharmacovigilance reporting system as well as provide systematic reporting to in\-house managers and external clients. * Dealing with external pharmaceutical departments and other multidisciplinary teams. * Maintain customer confidentiality at all times. * Assist in the development of pharmacovigilance procedures. * Act as a key information resource to pharmacovigilance team and other multidisciplinary teams. * In compliance with client requirements ensure that all service user records and files are managed, maintained and up to date * Create incidents reports that occur in PV and drive the actions that are required remediate errors. This may require liaising directly with other functional leads to align processes to PV standards. * Adhere to GVP (good pharmacovigilance practices) guidelines at all times. * Perform other duties as assigned by PV Manager * Represents self in a professional and ethical manner at all times, complying with all applicable company policy and procedures Role requirements: * Degree in life sciences or working towards, highly desirable. * High degree of accuracy with attention to detail essential. * Familiarity with good pharmacovigilance practices (GVP) desirable. * Knowledge of medical and drug terminology would be a distinct advantage. * Good written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work independently with moderate supervision. * Good knowledge and experience using MS Office would be beneficial. **What We Offer in Return:** Competitive annual salary 24 days of annual leave, rising with service Pension Scheme Life Assurance Health Insurance Income Protection Education Assistance funding with paid exam and study Leave Employee Discount Card **You Can Also Expect:** A supportive and friendly working environment Regular training courses and opportunities to up\-skill Promotional opportunities Refer a Friend scheme Social events Cycle to Work scheme

title: Platform Engineer
description: **Platform Engineer (AWS Cloud Infrastructure)** **12\-month Contract** **About the Company** Our client is a fast\-growing financial services organisation focused on building secure, resilient, and scalable cloud\-native platforms that support the next generation of digital financial products and services. Technology sits at the core of their business strategy, with significant investment in cloud infrastructure, automation, security, and engineering excellence. Operating within a highly regulated environment, the organisation is committed to delivering innovative solutions while maintaining the highest standards of security, governance, and operational resilience **.** **About the Role** We are seeking an experienced Platform Engineer to join a high\-performing cloud platform team responsible for designing, building, and supporting modern AWS\-based infrastructure environments. This is a hands\-on engineering role focused on cloud architecture, infrastructure automation, platform reliability, and security. The successful candidate will play a key role in building scalable cloud platforms, implementing Infrastructure as Code, and supporting secure software delivery pipelines. Working closely with engineering, security, and operations teams, you will help deliver well\-architected solutions that align with industry best practices and regulatory requirements. This position is ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex infrastructure challenges and contributing to the evolution of cloud\-native platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, build, and maintain secure, scalable, and resilient AWS cloud infrastructure * Implement and manage Infrastructure as Code (IaC) solutions using Terraform * Develop and maintain automation frameworks for cloud provisioning, deployment, and management * Support cloud networking, security, and governance initiatives across AWS environments * Design and manage multi\-account AWS architectures, landing zones, and cloud governance frameworks * Collaborate with engineering teams to build and optimise CI/CD pipelines and GitOps workflows * Implement security best practices including IAM, encryption, key management, and access controls * Monitor platform health, performance, reliability, and availability across cloud environments * Support incident management, troubleshooting, and root\-cause analysis activities * Contribute to cloud architecture decisions and platform strategy initiatives * Drive continuous improvement across automation, security, scalability, and operational processes * Work closely with security and compliance teams to ensure adherence to regulatory standards **Key Requirements** **Essential Skills \& Experience** * 5\+ years of experience in Platform Engineering, Cloud Engineering, Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), or Software Engineering * Minimum 3 years of hands\-on AWS experience * Proven experience designing and supporting production\-grade cloud infrastructure * Strong understanding of AWS networking, security, identity management, and governance * Advanced networking knowledge including firewalls, DNS, load balancing, proxies, and network security * Strong experience with Infrastructure as Code, particularly Terraform * Experience with CI/CD pipelines, automation frameworks, and GitOps methodologies * Proficiency in at least one scripting or programming language such as Python, Go, or Shell * Experience implementing cloud security best practices, including IAM, encryption, and KMS * Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across technical teams **Desirable Skills \& Experience** * Experience within financial services, fintech, insurance, or other regulated industries * Experience with multi\-account AWS environments, Control Tower, and landing zone implementations * Knowledge of Zero Trust security architectures * Hands\-on experience with Docker and Kubernetes/EKS * Experience with monitoring and observability tools such as Grafana, Prometheus, Splunk, Dynatrace, or AWS\-native services * Experience optimising cloud cost, performance, and operational efficiency * Exposure to cloud architecture and platform strategy initiatives * Participation in cloud engineering communities or open\-source projects

title: Platform Engineer
description: **About Smartbox:** We exist to be the go\-to companion, empowering everyone to transform their free time into experiences. At Smartbox Group, we believe that real\-life experiences help us thrive, connect, and become the best versions of ourselves. With nearly 15,000 different products across leisure themes — Stays, Wellness, Adventure, Gastronomy — we allow people to enrich their lives with moments of sharing, emotions, and unforgettable memories. Present in 11 countries, Smartbox Group employs over 700 people across Europe and works with more than 40,000 trusted partners, including hotels, restaurants, wellness centres, and outdoor activity providers. **Our Culture:** At Smartbox, we’re on a mission to unbox our world and unbox the potential of our People. We’re not just building a workplace — we’re cultivating a movement where bold ideas, authentic connections, and real\-world experiences redefine what work can be. We empower our people to take ownership, break boundaries, and dive headfirst into challenges that spark growth and impact. Together, we’re creating a culture of daring curiosity, relentless ambition, and transformative purpose — where every team member shapes their future and leaves a lasting mark on the world around us. **The Role:** We are looking for a Platform Engineer to join our Technical Operations team in Dublin, reporting to the Platform Engineering Manager. Platform Engineers at Smartbox are responsible for keeping all user\-facing and production systems running smoothly 24/7/365, constantly looking to improve workflows and processes to deliver best\-in\-class experiences to our customers and internal stakeholders. In this role, you will participate in our on\-call rotation, partner with development teams to improve their services and processes, and help maintain and advance our technology stack. **Key Responsibilities:** * Design, implement, and maintain cloud infrastructure in AWS using Terraform, including development of reusable modules. * Operate and enhance production Kubernetes clusters using Helm, Karpenter, ArgoCD, and AWS EKS. * Implement and maintain GitOps\-driven CI/CD pipelines for infrastructure and application deployments. * Improve platform reliability through proactive monitoring, alerting, and observability using Prometheus, Grafana, CheckMK, and internal tools. * Automate operational processes using Bash, Python, Go, and Rust. * Troubleshoot production incidents across infrastructure, networking, containers, and application layers. * Support and optimise large\-scale, database\-driven systems (MySQL required; PostgreSQL a plus). * Contribute to capacity planning, scaling strategies, and performance optimisation initiatives. * Drive operational excellence improvements and incident reduction initiatives. * Manage and optimise centralised logging using Elasticsearch, Filebeat, rsyslog, and Kibana. * Maintain documentation and participate in a 24x7 on\-call rotation. * Collaborate with development teams on tools development, service improvements, and change management activities. **About You:** We’re looking for someone who not only has the right skills but also embodies our guiding principles: * **The Owner** — You take accountability, act with integrity, and treat challenges as opportunities. * **The Ambitious Maverick** — You bring bold ideas, challenge the status quo, and are unafraid to take smart risks. * **The Radical Optimist** — You maintain a solutions\-focused mindset, inspiring others with your positivity and resilience. * **The Connector** — You build authentic relationships, foster collaboration, and create space for diverse voices to thrive. **Skills \& Experience:** **Essential:** * 3\+ years of experience in technical support, systems administration, or platform/infrastructure engineering. * Proficiency in LAMP/LNMP environments. * Hands\-on experience with Kubernetes and Docker. * Experience with modern CI/CD tooling (GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or equivalent). * Strong Linux systems knowledge and troubleshooting capability. * Solid understanding of networking fundamentals (DNS, TCP/IP, HTTP, load balancing, TLS, caching). * Experience with cloud automation on AWS and Terraform. * Experience with Elasticsearch and log management. * Scripting skills in Python, Bash, or similar languages. * Experience administering MySQL databases. * Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills. **Desirable:** * Experience with configuration management tools such as Chef/Cinc. * PostgreSQL database experience. * Experience leveraging AI tools to improve code quality and establishing AI\-related workflows. **What we offer:** 🏡 Hybrid work: 2–3 remote days \+ 20 days abroad per year ✅ Essentials: health cover, pension, taxsaver benefit, extra leave 🎁 Flexible benefits: personalize with health, wellbeing \& learning options 🌈 D\&I commitment: parental leave, Inclusive Day, gender equity in rewards L\&D Support: Study Assistance and Study Leave 🎉 Unbox Your World: Take 1 day leave per year as short notice (YOLO!) \+ paid sabbatical after 5 years 🌍 Transparency: clear, fair salary bands and career levels

title: Power Systems Engineer
description: At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** ELSE Cluster Manager North Europe \_\_ **Your role and responsibilities** In this role you will work as a power engineer on a range of exciting projects for global clients in a rewarding team environment, developing technical and solution development skills to develop into an industry\-recognized consulting engineer. The position would suit a practical and adaptable individual who wishes to work in a progressive, innovative electrical consultancy. We are seeking experienced Power Systems Engineers who are eager to deepen their technical expertise and contribute to complex engineering projects for global clients. This role is ideal for a practical, adaptable, and technically driven engineer who wants to grow into an industry\-recognized consulting specialist within a progressive and innovative electrical consultancy environment. The work model for the role is: \#hybrid, \#Li\-hybrid You will be mainly accountable for: * Providing technical input and leadership on a variety of complex projects. * Working closely with clients to understand their technical and business requirements and translating them into practical engineering solutions. * Ensuring project outputs meet client expectations, quality standards, timeframes, and budget requirements. * Conducting in\-depth power systems analysis using industry\-leading methodologies and software tools. * Preparing detailed technical and non\-technical reports, presenting clear recommendations and actionable insights. * Attending client meetings remotely, building trusted relationships and managing expectations through effective communication and responsiveness. * Tracking and reporting on project performance and client satisfaction. * Mentoring, coaching and developing more junior engineers. Online training to be delivered where applicable. **Typical projects include:** * Power system studies include load flow, short circuit, protection coordination and system resilience assessments. * Electrical safety and power quality studies, including arc flash, harmonics and earthing assessments. * Grid connection and renewable energy integration projects, covering stability, harmonics and compliance analyses. * Reliability assessments and failure investigations for critical electrical infrastructure. * Software\-based engineering solutions and emerging technology evaluations. * Supporting wind energy projects from design through installation and commissioning. **Qualifications for the role** * You are highly skilled in power system studies, including short circuit, protection coordination and arc flash analysis, and enjoy working on complex electrical infrastructure projects. * You have 3\+ years of experience in power systems engineering, applying technical expertise to deliver reliable, safe and efficient electrical solutions. * You possess advanced knowledge of industry\-leading power system analysis tools such as SKM, EasyPower, ETAP or similar. * You have proven experience in protection system design, settings development and coordination studies, with exposure to transformer and bus differential protection, REF and directional protection schemes. * You have experience performing electrical calculations and developing designs in accordance with relevant IEC standards. * Knowledge of power quality, harmonics and renewable energy integration studies would be an advantage. * You are passionate about solving technical challenges, collaborating with clients and stakeholders, and continuously expanding your expertise. * Degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field. * You are at ease communicating in English and can clearly present technical concepts to a wide range of audiences Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Primary Education Officer
description: Post\-Primary Languages Ireland (PPLI) are currently recruiting for a Primary Education Officer to join their team. **About The Role:** =================== Post\-Primary Languages Ireland/Languages Connect is seeking to recruit an Education Officer with competence in a foreign language to support the implementation of Languages Connect – Ireland’s Strategy for Foreign Languages in Education, diversifying, enhancing and expanding the teaching of foreign languages. The candidate for this role will be required to be qualified as a primary school teacher, have excellent leadership and interpersonal skills, be able to motivate and inspire teachers and work with teams of associates. Flexibility and an ability to engage in an evolving programme of work in a culture of improvement, collaboration, innovation, and creativity are required. **Key Requirements:** --------------------- Applicants must (on the latest date for receipt of completed applications): * Have in\-depth understanding of the Irish education system, the Languages Connect strategy and the Primary Language Curriculum * Hold a qualification to teach at primary level * Have relevant experience in primary level education * Have experience and expertise in the use of active learning methodologies and other up\-to\-date pedagogies and approaches to teaching and learning of languages * Have a proven record of managing complex projects, meeting deadlines, and achieving results * Possess a minimum level of CEFR B2 in at least one foreign language * Have excellent communication and presentation skills and the ability to write to a very high standard * Be proficient in Microsoft Office ### **Desirable Skills and Qualifications:** * Hold a qualification in a foreign language and/or a relevant qualification; * Irish language skills to carry out work through the medium of Irish; * Have familiarity with CEFR and CEFR Companion Volume ### **Required Competencies:** * Project management (including planning \& organisation, ability to multi\-task, prioritise where necessary, flexibility, working independently and as part of a team) * Judgement, analysis and decision making * Management and delivery of Results * Leadership, including interpersonal skills, motivating/inspiring others both within the team and with external stakeholders * Research and Report writing * Interpersonal and communication skills, including competence to engage stakeholders and partners * Professional knowledge, expertise, and self\-development, including digital expertise and capacity to prepare and deliver workshops, training and presentations **Key Responsibilities:** ------------------------- * Prepare detailed breakdown of deliverables and deadlines for projects * Oversee and manage the achievement of deliverables of projects * Manage, develop and enhance the Foreign Language Upskilling Scheme, by engaging with a range of providers, including Education Support Centres, HEIs, Cultural Institutes and Embassies * Evaluate the impact of programmes * Provide advice and training to tutors recruited to deliver upskilling courses, where relevant * Maintain effective communication with all course providers and teachers/schools * Deliver information sessions to hosting class teachers and school management teams in the context of Say Yes to Languages * Develop, prepare, and deliver resources and supports for teaching and learning to support delivery of MFL curriculum * Assess teachers’ needs via research and surveys * Establish and maintain working relationships with schools, Department of Education and all other relevant stakeholders * Manage training of, and collaboration with, associates * Support the delivery of messages to the wider public through articles, presentations and newsletter items. Prepare project reports and conduct evaluation of programmes * Represent PPLI/Languages Connect at events such as conferences, give presentations, facilitate workshops ***Deadline for applications is 4pm on Friday, 10th July 2026\.*** For more information on the role, please see the Information Booklet. To apply, please fill out the Application Form. **About PPLI:** =============== Post\-Primary Languages Ireland (PPLI) provides expertise and support for foreign language education in Ireland, fully funded by the Department of Education. Along with a growing number of partners, PPLI also delivers the Languages Connect awareness campaign, which highlights the benefits of foreign language skills.

title: Private Equity Investment Associate – Exceptional Platform & Career Development
description: **Job Title:** Private Equity Investment Associate – Exceptional Platform \& Career Development **Sector** : Private Equity **Location:** Dublin **Salary:** DOE plus bonus and benefits **Our Client** Our client is a highly regarded Private Equity investor with a long\-standing track record of partnering with ambitious businesses and supporting management teams through periods of significant growth and transformation. Due to continued investment activity and a strong deal pipeline, they are seeking to appoint an Investment Associate to join their Dublin team. **Why should you apply?** The role offers exposure across all aspects of the investment lifecycle, with Associates playing a hands\-on role from origination through to execution and portfolio value creation. Unlike many larger investment platforms, this team offers meaningful responsibility from day one. You will work directly with Partners and Investment Directors, gaining exposure to senior management teams and helping shape strategic decisions across a diverse portfolio of businesses. This is an outstanding opportunity for an ambitious individual looking to build a long\-term career in Private Equity within a collaborative and entrepreneurial environment. **Who should apply?** This opportunity is ideally suited to high\-performing professionals looking to make the move into Private Equity or individuals already operating within an investment environment seeking a platform with exceptional long\-term prospects. Experience in the following areas would be beneficial: • 3\-6 years' experience within Investment Banking, M\&A Advisory, Transaction Services, Corporate Finance or Private Equity. • Experience working on complex transactions and interacting with senior stakeholders. • Strong financial modelling, valuation and analytical capabilities. • Excellent academic credentials and a track record of progression. • Commercially minded with an intellectual curiosity and genuine interest in businesses and how they create value. • Strong communication skills and the ability to build relationships with management teams, advisers and investors. **Role and Reporting Lines** Working closely with Partners and Investment Directors, responsibilities will include: • Deal Execution – Support acquisitions and exits across the full transaction lifecycle. • Financial Analysis – Build and maintain detailed financial and valuation models. • Due Diligence – Coordinate financial, commercial and legal workstreams with external advisers. • Investment Committee Materials – Prepare investment papers and support key decision\-making processes. • Portfolio Engagement – Work alongside management teams to monitor performance and support growth initiatives. • Market Intelligence – Identify attractive sectors, themes and investment opportunities. • Stakeholder Interaction – Develop relationships with management teams, lenders and advisers. • Value Creation – Contribute to strategic initiatives across portfolio companies. **Interested in this position?** To apply please submit your CV to Lisa Tully who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further please contact us directly on 01 529 4200\.

title: Product Owner (Crypto)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** The Product team shapes the experiences that power Revolut. They turn complex problems into simple, scalable solutions — collaborating across Design, Engineering, and Data to build features our customers love. We’re looking for a Technical Product Owner to shape the architecture and feature set behind our crypto infrastructure and trading capabilities. You’ll own the roadmap for scalable, secure, and innovative crypto experiences for our customers. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Leading the full product lifecycle for crypto features and new products at a global scale while pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in fintech * Defining product requirements for secure, scalable systems * Driving integration with third\-party infrastructure, such as liquidity venues, custodians, and market data providers * Working cross\-functionally with Engineering, Risk and Compliance, and Treasury to build resilient, compliant features with real\-time financial and regulatory logic * Using data and user research to identify pain points, investigate anomalies, and deliver optimisations for pricing, latency, and trade UX * Supporting readiness for new asset classes, geographies, or regulatory regimes **What You'll Need** * 3\+ years of product ownership or technical product management experience in fintech, crypto, or financial infrastructure * A solid understanding of backend architecture and financial systems * Experience delivering high\-performance systems in regulated environments * Familiarity with crypto or traditional trading mechanics * Excellent collaboration skills and the ability to effectively align with multiple internal teams * Analytical thinking and proficiency with structured data and system design discussions **Nice to have** * Experience working with digital asset products in fintech and/or crypto * Familiarity with regulatory frameworks governing cryptoassets * Hands\-on experience with prototyping tools or user testing frameworks * An interest in behavioural design or gamified product mechanics Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Project Engineer
description: **Overview** **About RoviSys:** RoviSys is an independent system integrator with year\-over\-year growth in Ireland, Europe, and world\-wide. RoviSys provides a variety of information and automation services in many industries globally. RoviSys Ireland formed in 2022, with the Dublin Office open in May 2024\. Dublin is part of RoviSys Europe group and currently operates closely with our European HQ located in Utrecht in The Nederland’s. Manufactures and systems we are currently working on in Ireland are including but not limited to Emerson DeltaV, Rockwell, Siemens PCS7 and S7, Schneider, Honeywell IQSET and Trend PLCs. SCADA include, Ignition, Aveva, AVEVA PI, IQ Vision, Cimplicity, and iFIX. **About The Opportunity** Project Engineers are responsible for leading engineering teams that design and implement customer automation projects. Project engineers take a comprehensive approach to solving problems, learning the details of each project’s mechanical, electrical, and software systems. **Responsibilities** **Software Design \& Development:** * Leading design and develop control system software for PLCs, DCS, SCADA, MES and historian platforms. * Create and maintain detailed design documentation to support system architecture and functionality. * Assists Project Manager in identifying changes in project scope and developing change orders * Serves as a technical contact for customers **Hardware Design \& Testing** * Design electrical control panels and oversee their testing to ensure compliance with specifications and safety standards. **Project Execution \& Validation** * Lead and conduct Factory Acceptance Testing to validate system performance prior to deployment. * Lead site commissioning activities for both hardware and software components. * Perform Site Acceptance Testing to confirm system functionality in the live environment. * On site commissioning A Project Engineer will typically work in a project team of 2 – 20 and will be expected to work closely with the project manager. Site location can vary, currently are in Ireland, Netherlands, Germany, UK, Spain, Nordic’s and more across the EU. As RoviSys operates a platform\-independent model, we engage with a wide variety of industrial systems. This creates a unique opportunity to work in a diverse and fast\-paced environment where continuous development and growth are part of everyday work. **Qualifications** * Bachelor of Science (Level 8\) in Engineering, Engineering Technology, Computer Science or and equivalent qualification. * 5\+ years of experience in automation * Eligible to work and travel in Ireland and Europe without sponsorship. * Full Irish Drivers License * Attend the Dublin Office on a daily basis * Strong background in industrial automation and control systems. * Hands\-on experience with PLC/DCS programming and SCADA configuration. * Familiarity with validation protocols and quality standards in regulated industries. * Experience in leading a team of engineers in a project environment * Excellent problem\-solving ability * Strong communication skills. * As a minimum, you must be proficient on one or more of the following platforms: + Rockwell ControlLogix/CompactLogix, PlantPAx, or FactoryTalk + Emerson DeltaV + Schneider Electric Modicon PLCs, EcoStruxure, PowerSCADA, Power Monitoring Expert (PME) + Siemens S7, PCS7, or WinCC + Ignition + Or similar IT, automation, or MES platform Open to travel is a must.

title: Quality Assurance Engineer
description: **Tech that takes you places** We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together. As a QA Engineer, you will help drive the delivery of high\-quality, reliable software across front\-end and back\-end systems. Working within a collaborative Agile team, you will design and execute manual test plans, identify defects and risks, and champion quality throughout the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC). You will work closely with engineers, product managers, designers, and operational teams to ensure quality is embedded from requirements through to release. This role requires strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and a proactive mindset focused on continuous improvement and delivering an excellent customer experience. **Your Journey starts here!** **What you’ll do as a QAEngineer** * Collaborate with Project Managers, Engineers, Product Managers, and Designers to ensure quality is embedded throughout the software development lifecycle. * Analyse business and technical requirements from multiple stakeholder perspectives, including end\-users, partners, operations, and customer support teams. * Design, develop, and execute detailed manual test cases, test scenarios, and regression test plans. * Support the development and maintenance of automated tests to improve software quality, regression coverage, and release confidence. * Identify, document, prioritise, and track defects through to resolution using tools such as Jira. * Support bug triage, escalation management, and root cause analysis activities. * Assess delivery risk and determine appropriate testing scope, coverage, and priorities. * Validate functional and non\-functional requirements across front\-end and back\-end systems. * Monitor and report on testing progress, quality metrics, risks, issues, and dependencies to relevant stakeholders. * Work closely with cross\-functional teams to ensure timely and high\-quality product releases. * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across QA processes, standards, methodologies, and tooling. * Support knowledge sharing and mentor junior QA team members where appropriate. * Advocate for best practices in software quality, usability, and customer experience. * Operate effectively within a fast\-paced, collaborative Agile environment. **What you’ll bring to the team** * 3\+ years of experience in Software Quality Assurance or a related testing role. * ISTQB Foundation Certification or equivalent software testing certification is desirable. * Experience working within Agile/Scrum or iterative software development environments. * Strong understanding of software testing principles, methodologies, and defect management processes. * Experience creating and executing manual test plans, test cases, and regression testing activities. * Experience developing, executing, and maintaining automated test suites using modern test automation frameworks and tools. * Familiarity with tools such as Jira, Confluence, Excel, or similar QA/project management tools. * Experience testing web applications, APIs, and back\-end services is advantageous. * Ability to analyse complex requirements and identify potential risks and edge cases. * Exposure to test automation frameworks or AI\-assisted testing tools is advantageous. * Understanding of modern QA practices including automation, CI/CD, and shift\-left testing principles is desirable * Strong organisational, prioritisation, and time management skills. * Ability to work independently while managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. * Excellent analytical thinking and problem\-solving capabilities. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to delivering high\-quality outcomes. **Go on, give it a go!** We know that looking for a new job can feel a bit daunting but don’t let a touch of imposter syndrome hold you back. Even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you! Apply today to stay connected. At CarTrawler, we’re proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone. No matter your background, identity, or life experience, if you’re passionate about what you do, you’ll be welcomed here.

title: Quality Control Laboratory Apprentice
description: By clicking the “Apply” button, I understand that my employment application process with Takeda will commence and that the information I provide in my application will be processed in line with Takeda’s Privacy Notice and Terms of Use. Then take your future in your hands and become part of Takeda as a **Quality Control Laboratory Apprentice:** This role offer unique opportunity to gain hands\-on experience and comprehensive training in Quality Control (QC) practices within a cutting\-edge pharmaceutical environment. Joining the team allows the applicant to develop crucial technical and soft skills while contributing to Takeda’s commitment to innovation, quality, and patient\-centric values. This apprenticeship fosters professional growth, teamwork, and meaningful work that impacts global healthcare. **You will be trained to possess the following skills :** * Demonstrating the ability to operate equipment and instruments effectively and according to standard operating procedures (SOPs). * Showing the ability to work effectively in a safe manner within the laboratory. * Communicating the theoretical knowledge of relevant sciences and technology. * Handling materials and equipment correctly paying due regard to safety and environmental protection regulations. * Capability to
